verses.bsb: He gave him no inheritance here, not even a foot of ground. But God promised to give possession of the land to Abraham and his descendants, even though he did not yet have a child.

verses.esv: And God spoke to this effect—that his offspring would be sojourners in a land belonging to others, who would enslave them and afflict them four hundred years.
verses.kjv: And God spake on this wise, That his seed should sojourn in a strange land; and that they should bring them into bondage, and entreat them evil four hundred years.
verses.bsb: God told him that his descendants would be foreigners in a strange land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated four hundred years.

verses.esv: ‘But I will judge the nation that they serve,’ said God, ‘and after that they shall come out and worship me in this place.’
verses.kjv: And the nation to whom they shall be in bondage will I judge, said God: and after that shall they come forth, and serve me in this place.
verses.bsb: ‘But I will punish the nation that enslaves them,’ God said, ‘and afterward they will come forth and worship Me in this place.’

verses.esv: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision. And so Abraham became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him on the eighth day, and Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob of the twelve patriarchs.
verses.kjv: And he gave him the covenant of circumcision: and so Abraham begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat the twelve patriarchs.
verses.bsb: Then God gave Abraham the covenant of circumcision, and Abraham became the father of Isaac and circumcised him on the eighth day. And Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob of the twelve patriarchs.

verses.esv: “And the patriarchs, jealous of Joseph, sold him into Egypt; but God was with him
verses.kjv: And the patriarchs, moved with envy, sold Joseph into Egypt: but God was with him,
verses.bsb: Because the patriarchs were jealous of Joseph, they sold him as a slave into Egypt. But God was with him

verses.esv: and rescued him out of all his afflictions and gave him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who made him ruler over Egypt and over all his household.
verses.kjv: And delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he made him governor over Egypt and all his house.
verses.bsb: and rescued him from all his troubles. He granted Joseph favor and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt, who appointed him ruler over Egypt and all his household.

verses.esv: Now there came a famine throughout all Egypt and Canaan, and great affliction, and our fathers could find no food.
verses.kjv: Now there came a dearth over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great affliction: and our fathers found no sustenance.
verses.bsb: Then famine and great suffering swept across Egypt and Canaan, and our fathers could not find food.

verses.esv: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent out our fathers on their first visit.
verses.kjv: But when Jacob heard that there was corn in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.
verses.bsb: When Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our fathers on their first visit.

verses.esv: And on the second visit Joseph made himself known to his brothers, and Joseph's family became known to Pharaoh.
verses.kjv: And at the second time Joseph was made known to his brethren; and Joseph’s kindred was made known unto Pharaoh.
verses.bsb: On their second visit, Joseph revealed his identity to his brothers, and his family became known to Pharaoh.

verses.esv: And Joseph sent and summoned Jacob his father and all his kindred, seventy-five persons in all.
verses.kjv: Then sent Joseph, and called his father Jacob to him, and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.
verses.bsb: Then Joseph sent for his father Jacob and all his relatives, seventy-five in all.

verses.esv: And Jacob went down into Egypt, and he died, he and our fathers,
verses.kjv: So Jacob went down into Egypt, and died, he, and our fathers,
verses.bsb: So Jacob went down to Egypt, where he and our fathers died.

verses.esv: and they were carried back to Shechem and laid in the tomb that Abraham had bought for a sum of silver from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
verses.kjv: And were carried over into Sychem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor the father of Sychem.
verses.bsb: Their bones were carried back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor at Shechem for a price he paid in silver.

verses.esv: “But as the time of the promise drew near, which God had granted to Abraham, the people increased and multiplied in Egypt
verses.kjv: But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
verses.bsb: As the time drew near for God to fulfill His promise to Abraham, our people in Egypt increased greatly in number.

verses.esv: until there arose over Egypt another king who did not know Joseph.
verses.kjv: Till another king arose, which knew not Joseph.
verses.bsb: Then another king, who knew nothing of Joseph, arose over Egypt.

verses.esv: He dealt shrewdly with our race and forced our fathers to expose their infants, so that they would not be kept alive.
verses.kjv: The same dealt subtilly with our kindred, and evil entreated our fathers, so that they cast out their young children, to the end they might not live.
verses.bsb: He exploited our people and oppressed our fathers, forcing them to abandon their infants so they would die.

verses.esv: At this time Moses was born; and he was beautiful in God's sight. And he was brought up for three months in his father's house,
verses.kjv: In which time Moses was born, and was exceeding fair, and nourished up in his father’s house three months:
verses.bsb: At that time Moses was born, and he was beautiful in the sight of God. For three months he was nurtured in his father’s house.

verses.esv: and when he was exposed, Pharaoh's daughter adopted him and brought him up as her own son.
verses.kjv: And when he was cast out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own son.
verses.bsb: When he was set outside, Pharaoh’s daughter took him and brought him up as her own son.

verses.esv: And Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and he was mighty in his words and deeds.
verses.kjv: And Moses was learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.
verses.bsb: So Moses was educated in all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in speech and action.

verses.esv: “When he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brothers, the children of Israel.
verses.kjv: And when he was full forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brethren the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: When Moses was forty years old, he decided to visit his brothers, the children of Israel.

verses.esv: And seeing one of them being wronged, he defended the oppressed man and avenged him by striking down the Egyptian.
verses.kjv: And seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him that was oppressed, and smote the Egyptian:
verses.bsb: And when he saw one of them being mistreated, Moses went to his defense and avenged him by striking down the Egyptian who was oppressing him.

verses.esv: He supposed that his brothers would understand that God was giving them salvation by his hand, but they did not understand.
verses.kjv: For he supposed his brethren would have understood how that God by his hand would deliver them: but they understood not.
verses.bsb: He assumed his brothers would understand that God was using him to deliver them, but they did not.

verses.esv: And on the following day he appeared to them as they were quarreling and tried to reconcile them, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers. Why do you wrong each other?’
verses.kjv: And the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove, and would have set them at one again, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren; why do ye wrong one to another?
verses.bsb: The next day he came upon two Israelites who were fighting, and he tried to reconcile them, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers. Why are you mistreating each other?’

verses.esv: But the man who was wronging his neighbor thrust him aside, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge over us?
verses.kjv: But he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge over us?
verses.bsb: But the man who was abusing his neighbor pushed Moses aside and said, ‘Who made you ruler and judge over us?

verses.esv: Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’
verses.kjv: Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?
verses.bsb: Do you want to kill me as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’

verses.esv: At this retort Moses fled and became an exile in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.
verses.kjv: Then fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begat two sons.
verses.bsb: At this remark, Moses fled to the land of Midian, where he lived as a foreigner and had two sons.

verses.esv: “Now when forty years had passed, an angel appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in a flame of fire in a bush.
verses.kjv: And when forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sina an angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.
verses.bsb: After forty years had passed, an angel appeared to Moses in the flames of a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.

verses.esv: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight, and as he drew near to look, there came the voice of the Lord:
verses.kjv: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight: and as he drew near to behold it, the voice of the Lord came unto him,
verses.bsb: When Moses saw it, he marveled at the sight. As he approached to look more closely, the voice of the Lord came to him:

verses.esv: ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham and of Isaac and of Jacob.’ And Moses trembled and did not dare to look.
verses.kjv: Saying, I am the God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.
verses.bsb: ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.’ Moses trembled with fear and did not dare to look.

verses.esv: Then the Lord said to him, ‘Take off the sandals from your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.
verses.kjv: Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.
verses.bsb: Then the Lord said to him, ‘Take off your sandals, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.

verses.esv: I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt, and have heard their groaning, and I have come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send you to Egypt.’
verses.kjv: I have seen, I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and am come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.
verses.bsb: I have indeed seen the oppression of My people in Egypt. I have heard their groaning and have come down to deliver them. Now come, I will send you back to Egypt.’

verses.esv: “This Moses, whom they rejected, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge?’—this man God sent as both ruler and redeemer by the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
verses.kjv: This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge? the same did God send to be a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush.
verses.bsb: This Moses, whom they had rejected with the words, ‘Who made you ruler and judge?’ is the one whom God sent to be their ruler and redeemer through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

verses.esv: This man led them out, performing wonders and signs in Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the wilderness for forty years.
verses.kjv: He brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.
verses.bsb: He led them out and performed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, at the Red Sea, and for forty years in the wilderness.

verses.esv: This is the Moses who said to the Israelites, ‘God will raise up for you a prophet like me from your brothers.’
verses.kjv: ¶ This is that Moses, which said unto the children of Israel, A prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear.
verses.bsb: This is the same Moses who told the Israelites, ‘God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers.’

verses.esv: This is the one who was in the congregation in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him at Mount Sinai, and with our fathers. He received living oracles to give to us.
verses.kjv: This is he, that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount Sina, and with our fathers: who received the lively oracles to give unto us:
verses.bsb: He was in the assembly in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and with our fathers. And he received living words to pass on to us.

verses.esv: Our fathers refused to obey him, but thrust him aside, and in their hearts they turned to Egypt,
verses.kjv: To whom our fathers would not obey, but thrust him from them, and in their hearts turned back again into Egypt,
verses.bsb: But our fathers refused to obey him. Instead, they rejected him and in their hearts turned back to Egypt.

verses.esv: saying to Aaron, ‘Make for us gods who will go before us. As for this Moses who led us out from the land of Egypt, we do not know what has become of him.’
verses.kjv: Saying unto Aaron, Make us gods to go before us: for as for this Moses, which brought us out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
verses.bsb: They said to Aaron, ‘Make us gods who will go before us! As for this Moses who led us out of the land of Egypt, we do not know what has happened to him.’

verses.esv: And they made a calf in those days, and offered a sacrifice to the idol and were rejoicing in the works of their hands.
verses.kjv: And they made a calf in those days, and offered sacrifice unto the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.
verses.bsb: At that time they made a calf and offered a sacrifice to the idol, rejoicing in the works of their hands.

verses.esv: But God turned away and gave them over to worship the host of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets: “‘Did you bring to me slain beasts and sacrifices, during the forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?
verses.kjv: Then God turned, and gave them up to worship the host of heaven; as it is written in the book of the prophets, O ye house of Israel, have ye offered to me slain beasts and sacrifices by the space of forty years in the wilderness?
verses.bsb: But God turned away from them and gave them over to the worship of the host of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets:  ‘Did you bring Me sacrifices and offerings forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?

verses.esv: You took up the tent of Moloch and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made to worship; and I will send you into exile beyond Babylon.’
verses.kjv: Yea, ye took up the tabernacle of Moloch, and the star of your god Remphan, figures which ye made to worship them: and I will carry you away beyond Babylon.
verses.bsb: You have taken along the tabernacle of Molech and the star of your god Rephan, the idols you made to worship. Therefore I will send you into exile beyond Babylon.’

verses.esv: “Our fathers had the tent of witness in the wilderness, just as he who spoke to Moses directed him to make it, according to the pattern that he had seen.
verses.kjv: Our fathers had the tabernacle of witness in the wilderness, as he had appointed, speaking unto Moses, that he should make it according to the fashion that he had seen.
verses.bsb: Our fathers had the tabernacle of the Testimony with them in the wilderness. It was constructed exactly as God had directed Moses, according to the pattern he had seen.

verses.esv: Our fathers in turn brought it in with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations that God drove out before our fathers. So it was until the days of David,
verses.kjv: Which also our fathers that came after brought in with Jesus into the possession of the Gentiles, whom God drave out before the face of our fathers, unto the days of David;
verses.bsb: And our fathers who received it brought it in with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations God drove out before them. It remained until the time of David,

verses.esv: who found favor in the sight of God and asked to find a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
verses.kjv: Who found favour before God, and desired to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
verses.bsb: who found favor in the sight of God and asked to provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.

verses.esv: But it was Solomon who built a house for him.
verses.kjv: But Solomon built him an house.
verses.bsb: But it was Solomon who built the house for Him.

verses.esv: Yet the Most High does not dwell in houses made by hands, as the prophet says,
verses.kjv: Howbeit the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands; as saith the prophet,
verses.bsb: However, the Most High does not dwell in houses made by human hands. As the prophet says:

verses.esv: “‘Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or what is the place of my rest?
verses.kjv: Heaven is my throne, and earth is my footstool: what house will ye build me? saith the Lord: or what is the place of my rest?
verses.bsb: ‘Heaven is My throne and the earth is My footstool. What kind of house will you build for Me, says the Lord, or where will My place of repose be?

verses.esv: Did not my hand make all these things?’
verses.kjv: Hath not my hand made all these things?
verses.bsb: Has not My hand made all these things?’

verses.esv: “You stiff-necked people, uncircumcised in heart and ears, you always resist the Holy Spirit. As your fathers did, so do you.
verses.kjv: ¶ Ye stiffnecked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, ye do always resist the Holy Ghost: as your fathers did, so do ye.
verses.bsb: You stiff-necked people with uncircumcised hearts and ears! You always resist the Holy Spirit, just as your fathers did.

verses.esv: Which of the prophets did your fathers not persecute? And they killed those who announced beforehand the coming of the Righteous One, whom you have now betrayed and murdered,
verses.kjv: Which of the prophets have not your fathers persecuted? and they have slain them which shewed before of the coming of the Just One; of whom ye have been now the betrayers and murderers:
verses.bsb: Which of the prophets did your fathers fail to persecute? They even killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One. And now you are His betrayers and murderers—

verses.esv: you who received the law as delivered by angels and did not keep it.”
verses.kjv: Who have received the law by the disposition of angels, and have not kept it.
verses.bsb: you who received the law ordained by angels, yet have not kept it.”

verses.esv: Now when they heard these things they were enraged, and they ground their teeth at him.
verses.kjv: ¶ When they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed on him with their teeth.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, the members of the Sanhedrin were enraged, and they gnashed their teeth at him.

verses.esv: But he, full of the Holy Spirit, gazed into heaven and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.
verses.kjv: But he, being full of the Holy Ghost, looked up stedfastly into heaven, and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,
verses.bsb: But Stephen, full of the Holy Spirit, looked intently into heaven and saw the glory of God and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.

verses.esv: And he said, “Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.”
verses.kjv: And said, Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God.
verses.bsb: “Look,” he said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God.”

verses.esv: But they cried out with a loud voice and stopped their ears and rushed together at him.
verses.kjv: Then they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their ears, and ran upon him with one accord,
verses.bsb: At this they covered their ears, cried out in a loud voice, and rushed together at him.

verses.esv: Then they cast him out of the city and stoned him. And the witnesses laid down their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.
verses.kjv: And cast him out of the city, and stoned him: and the witnesses laid down their clothes at a young man’s feet, whose name was Saul.
verses.bsb: They dragged him out of the city and began to stone him. Meanwhile the witnesses laid their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.

verses.esv: And as they were stoning Stephen, he called out, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.”
verses.kjv: And they stoned Stephen, calling upon God, and saying, Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.
verses.bsb: While they were stoning him, Stephen appealed, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.”

verses.esv: And falling to his knees he cried out with a loud voice, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them.” And when he had said this, he fell asleep.
verses.kjv: And he kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, Lord, lay not this sin to their charge. And when he had said this, he fell asleep.
verses.bsb: Falling on his knees, he cried out in a loud voice, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them.” And when he had said this, he fell asleep.

verses.esv: And Saul approved of his execution. And there arose on that day a great persecution against the church in Jerusalem, and they were all scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except the apostles.
verses.kjv: AND Saul was consenting unto his death. And at that time there was a great persecution against the church which was at Jerusalem; and they were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judæa and Samaria, except the apostles.
verses.bsb: And Saul was there, giving approval to Stephen’s death.  On that day a great persecution broke out against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were scattered throughout Judea and Samaria.

verses.esv: Devout men buried Stephen and made great lamentation over him.
verses.kjv: And devout men carried Stephen to his burial, and made great lamentation over him.
verses.bsb: God-fearing men buried Stephen and mourned deeply over him.

verses.esv: But Saul was ravaging the church, and entering house after house, he dragged off men and women and committed them to prison.
verses.kjv: As for Saul, he made havock of the church, entering into every house, and haling men and women committed them to prison.
verses.bsb: But Saul began to destroy the church. Going from house to house, he dragged off men and women and put them in prison.

verses.esv: Now those who were scattered went about preaching the word.
verses.kjv: Therefore they that were scattered abroad went every where preaching the word.
verses.bsb: Those who had been scattered preached the word wherever they went.

verses.esv: Philip went down to the city of Samaria and proclaimed to them the Christ.
verses.kjv: Then Philip went down to the city of Samaria, and preached Christ unto them.
verses.bsb: Philip went down to a city in Samaria and proclaimed the Christ to them.

verses.esv: And the crowds with one accord paid attention to what was being said by Philip, when they heard him and saw the signs that he did.
verses.kjv: And the people with one accord gave heed unto those things which Philip spake, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.
verses.bsb: The crowds gave their undivided attention to Philip’s message and to the signs they saw him perform.

verses.esv: For unclean spirits, crying out with a loud voice, came out of many who had them, and many who were paralyzed or lame were healed.
verses.kjv: For unclean spirits, crying with loud voice, came out of many that were possessed with them: and many taken with palsies, and that were lame, were healed.
verses.bsb: With loud shrieks, unclean spirits came out of many who were possessed, and many of the paralyzed and lame were healed.

verses.esv: So there was much joy in that city.
verses.kjv: And there was great joy in that city.
verses.bsb: So there was great joy in that city.

verses.esv: But there was a man named Simon, who had previously practiced magic in the city and amazed the people of Samaria, saying that he himself was somebody great.
verses.kjv: But there was a certain man, called Simon, which beforetime in the same city used sorcery, and bewitched the people of Samaria, giving out that himself was some great one:
verses.bsb: Prior to that time, a man named Simon had practiced sorcery in the city and astounded the people of Samaria. He claimed to be someone great,

verses.esv: They all paid attention to him, from the least to the greatest, saying, “This man is the power of God that is called Great.”
verses.kjv: To whom they all gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.
verses.bsb: and all the people, from the least to the greatest, heeded his words and said, “This man is the divine power called the Great Power.”

verses.esv: And they paid attention to him because for a long time he had amazed them with his magic.
verses.kjv: And to him they had regard, because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries.
verses.bsb: They paid close attention to him because he had astounded them for a long time with his sorcery.

verses.esv: But when they believed Philip as he preached good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
verses.kjv: But when they believed Philip preaching the things concerning the kingdom of God, and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.
verses.bsb: But when they believed Philip as he preached the gospel of the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized, both men and women.

verses.esv: Even Simon himself believed, and after being baptized he continued with Philip. And seeing signs and great miracles performed, he was amazed.
verses.kjv: Then Simon himself believed also: and when he was baptized, he continued with Philip, and wondered, beholding the miracles and signs which were done.
verses.bsb: Even Simon himself believed and was baptized. He followed Philip closely and was astounded by the great signs and miracles he observed.

verses.esv: Now when the apostles at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent to them Peter and John,
verses.kjv: Now when the apostles which were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent unto them Peter and John:
verses.bsb: When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them.

verses.esv: who came down and prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit,
verses.kjv: Who, when they were come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Ghost:
verses.bsb: On their arrival, they prayed for them to receive the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: for he had not yet fallen on any of them, but they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
verses.kjv: (For as yet he was fallen upon none of them: only they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.)
verses.bsb: For the Holy Spirit had not yet fallen upon any of them; they had simply been baptized into the name of the Lord Jesus.

verses.esv: Then they laid their hands on them and they received the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: Then laid they their hands on them, and they received the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: Then Peter and John laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: Now when Simon saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles' hands, he offered them money,
verses.kjv: And when Simon saw that through laying on of the apostles’ hands the Holy Ghost was given, he offered them money,
verses.bsb: When Simon saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money.

verses.esv: saying, “Give me this power also, so that anyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”
verses.kjv: Saying, Give me also this power, that on whomsoever I lay hands, he may receive the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: “Give me this power as well,” he said, “so that everyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”

verses.esv: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain the gift of God with money!
verses.kjv: But Peter said unto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the gift of God may be purchased with money.
verses.bsb: But Peter replied, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could buy the gift of God with money!

verses.esv: You have neither part nor lot in this matter, for your heart is not right before God.
verses.kjv: Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter: for thy heart is not right in the sight of God.
verses.bsb: You have no part or share in our ministry, because your heart is not right before God.

verses.esv: Repent, therefore, of this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord that, if possible, the intent of your heart may be forgiven you.
verses.kjv: Repent therefore of this thy wickedness, and pray God, if perhaps the thought of thine heart may be forgiven thee.
verses.bsb: Repent, therefore, of your wickedness, and pray to the Lord. Perhaps He will forgive you for the intent of your heart.

verses.esv: For I see that you are in the gall of bitterness and in the bond of iniquity.”
verses.kjv: For I perceive that thou art in the gall of bitterness, and in the bond of iniquity.
verses.bsb: For I see that you are poisoned by bitterness and captive to iniquity.”

verses.esv: And Simon answered, “Pray for me to the Lord, that nothing of what you have said may come upon me.”
verses.kjv: Then answered Simon, and said, Pray ye to the Lord for me, that none of these things which ye have spoken come upon me.
verses.bsb: Then Simon answered, “Pray to the Lord for me, so that nothing you have said may happen to me.”

verses.esv: Now when they had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the gospel to many villages of the Samaritans.
verses.kjv: And they, when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.
verses.bsb: And after Peter and John had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the gospel in many of the Samaritan villages.

verses.esv: Now an angel of the Lord said to Philip, “Rise and go toward the south to the road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.” This is a desert place.
verses.kjv: And the angel of the Lord spake unto Philip, saying, Arise, and go toward the south unto the way that goeth down from Jerusalem unto Gaza, which is desert.
verses.bsb: Now an angel of the Lord said to Philip, “Get up and go south to the desert road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.”

verses.esv: And he rose and went. And there was an Ethiopian, a eunuch, a court official of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was in charge of all her treasure. He had come to Jerusalem to worship
verses.kjv: And he arose and went: and, behold, a man of Ethiopia, an eunuch of great authority under Candace queen of the Ethiopians, who had the charge of all her treasure, and had come to Jerusalem for to worship,
verses.bsb: So he started out, and on his way he met an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official in charge of the entire treasury of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians. He had gone to Jerusalem to worship,

verses.esv: and was returning, seated in his chariot, and he was reading the prophet Isaiah.
verses.kjv: Was returning, and sitting in his chariot read Esaias the prophet.
verses.bsb: and on his return was sitting in his chariot reading Isaiah the prophet.

verses.esv: And the Spirit said to Philip, “Go over and join this chariot.”
verses.kjv: Then the Spirit said unto Philip, Go near, and join thyself to this chariot.
verses.bsb: The Spirit said to Philip, “Go over to that chariot and stay by it.”

verses.esv: So Philip ran to him and heard him reading Isaiah the prophet and asked, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
verses.kjv: And Philip ran thither to him, and heard him read the prophet Esaias, and said, Understandest thou what thou readest?
verses.bsb: So Philip ran up and heard the man reading Isaiah the prophet. “Do you understand what you are reading?” Philip asked.

verses.esv: And he said, “How can I, unless someone guides me?” And he invited Philip to come up and sit with him.
verses.kjv: And he said, How can I, except some man should guide me? And he desired Philip that he would come up and sit with him.
verses.bsb: “How can I,” he said, “unless someone guides me?” And he invited Philip to come up and sit with him.

verses.esv: Now the passage of the Scripture that he was reading was this: “Like a sheep he was led to the slaughter and like a lamb before its shearer is silent, so he opens not his mouth.
verses.kjv: The place of the scripture which he read was this, He was led as a sheep to the slaughter; and like a lamb dumb before his shearer, so opened he not his mouth:
verses.bsb: The eunuch was reading this passage of Scripture:  “He was led like a sheep to the slaughter, and as a lamb before the shearer is silent, so He did not open His mouth.

verses.esv: In his humiliation justice was denied him. Who can describe his generation? For his life is taken away from the earth.”
verses.kjv: In his humiliation his judgment was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? for his life is taken from the earth.
verses.bsb: In His humiliation He was deprived of justice. Who can recount His descendants? For His life was removed from the earth.”

verses.esv: And the eunuch said to Philip, “About whom, I ask you, does the prophet say this, about himself or about someone else?”
verses.kjv: And the eunuch answered Philip, and said, I pray thee, of whom speaketh the prophet this? of himself, or of some other man?
verses.bsb: “Tell me,” said the eunuch, “who is the prophet talking about, himself or someone else?”

verses.esv: Then Philip opened his mouth, and beginning with this Scripture he told him the good news about Jesus.
verses.kjv: Then Philip opened his mouth, and began at the same scripture, and preached unto him Jesus.
verses.bsb: Then Philip began with this very Scripture and told him the good news about Jesus.

verses.esv: And as they were going along the road they came to some water, and the eunuch said, “See, here is water! What prevents me from being baptized?”
verses.kjv: And as they went on their way, they came unto a certain water: and the eunuch said, See, here is water; what doth hinder me to be baptized?
verses.bsb: As they traveled along the road and came to some water, the eunuch said, “Look, here is water! What is there to prevent me from being baptized?”

verses.esv: And he commanded the chariot to stop, and they both went down into the water, Philip and the eunuch, and he baptized him.
verses.kjv: And he commanded the chariot to stand still: and they went down both into the water, both Philip and the eunuch; and he baptized him.
verses.bsb: And he gave orders to stop the chariot. Then both Philip and the eunuch went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.

verses.esv: And when they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord carried Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more, and went on his way rejoicing.
verses.kjv: And when they were come up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord caught away Philip, that the eunuch saw him no more: and he went on his way rejoicing.
verses.bsb: When they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord carried Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more, but went on his way rejoicing.

verses.esv: But Philip found himself at Azotus, and as he passed through he preached the gospel to all the towns until he came to Caesarea.
verses.kjv: But Philip was found at Azotus: and passing through he preached in all the cities, till he came to Cæsarea.
verses.bsb: But Philip appeared at Azotus and traveled through that region, preaching the gospel in all the towns until he came to Caesarea.

verses.esv: But Saul, still breathing threats and murder against the disciples of the Lord, went to the high priest
verses.kjv: AND Saul, yet breathing out threatenings and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, went unto the high priest,
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, Saul was still breathing out murderous threats against the disciples of the Lord. He approached the high priest

verses.esv: and asked him for letters to the synagogues at Damascus, so that if he found any belonging to the Way, men or women, he might bring them bound to Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: And desired of him letters to Damascus to the synagogues, that if he found any of this way, whether they were men or women, he might bring them bound unto Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: and requested letters to the synagogues in Damascus, so that if he found any men or women belonging to the Way, he could bring them as prisoners to Jerusalem.

verses.esv: Now as he went on his way, he approached Damascus, and suddenly a light from heaven shone around him.
verses.kjv: And as he journeyed, he came near Damascus: and suddenly there shined round about him a light from heaven:
verses.bsb: As Saul drew near to Damascus on his journey, suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him.

verses.esv: And falling to the ground, he heard a voice saying to him, “Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?”
verses.kjv: And he fell to the earth, and heard a voice saying unto him, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
verses.bsb: He fell to the ground and heard a voice say to him, “Saul, Saul, why do you persecute Me?”

verses.esv: And he said, “Who are you, Lord?” And he said, “I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.
verses.kjv: And he said, Who art thou, Lord? And the Lord said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest: it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.
verses.bsb: “Who are You, Lord?” Saul asked.  “I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting,” He replied.

verses.esv: But rise and enter the city, and you will be told what you are to do.”
verses.kjv: And he trembling and astonished said, Lord, what wilt thou have me to do? And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.
verses.bsb: “Now get up and go into the city, and you will be told what you must do.”

verses.esv: The men who were traveling with him stood speechless, hearing the voice but seeing no one.
verses.kjv: And the men which journeyed with him stood speechless, hearing a voice, but seeing no man.
verses.bsb: The men traveling with Saul stood there speechless. They heard the voice but did not see anyone.

verses.esv: Saul rose from the ground, and although his eyes were opened, he saw nothing. So they led him by the hand and brought him into Damascus.
verses.kjv: And Saul arose from the earth; and when his eyes were opened, he saw no man: but they led him by the hand, and brought him into Damascus.
verses.bsb: Saul got up from the ground, but when he opened his eyes he could not see a thing. So they led him by the hand into Damascus.

verses.esv: And for three days he was without sight, and neither ate nor drank.
verses.kjv: And he was three days without sight, and neither did eat nor drink.
verses.bsb: For three days he was without sight, and he did not eat or drink anything.

verses.esv: Now there was a disciple at Damascus named Ananias. The Lord said to him in a vision, “Ananias.” And he said, “Here I am, Lord.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And there was a certain disciple at Damascus, named Ananias; and to him said the Lord in a vision, Ananias. And he said, Behold, I am here, Lord.
verses.bsb: In Damascus there was a disciple named Ananias. The Lord spoke to him in a vision, “Ananias!”  “Here I am, Lord,” he answered.

verses.esv: And the Lord said to him, “Rise and go to the street called Straight, and at the house of Judas look for a man of Tarsus named Saul, for behold, he is praying,
verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the street which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Judas for one called Saul, of Tarsus: for, behold, he prayeth,
verses.bsb: “Get up!” the Lord told him. “Go to the house of Judas on Straight Street and ask for a man from Tarsus named Saul, for he is praying.

verses.esv: and he has seen in a vision a man named Ananias come in and lay his hands on him so that he might regain his sight.”
verses.kjv: And hath seen in a vision a man named Ananias coming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receive his sight.
verses.bsb: In a vision he has seen a man named Ananias come and place his hands on him to restore his sight.”

verses.esv: But Ananias answered, “Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much evil he has done to your saints at Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: Then Ananias answered, Lord, I have heard by many of this man, how much evil he hath done to thy saints at Jerusalem:
verses.bsb: But Ananias answered, “Lord, many people have told me about this man and all the harm he has done to Your saints in Jerusalem.

verses.esv: And here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who call on your name.”
verses.kjv: And here he hath authority from the chief priests to bind all that call on thy name.
verses.bsb: And now he is here with authority from the chief priests to arrest all who call on Your name.”

verses.esv: But the Lord said to him, “Go, for he is a chosen instrument of mine to carry my name before the Gentiles and kings and the children of Israel.
verses.kjv: But the Lord said unto him, Go thy way: for he is a chosen vessel unto me, to bear my name before the Gentiles, and kings, and the children of Israel:
verses.bsb: “Go!” said the Lord. “This man is My chosen instrument to carry My name before the Gentiles and their kings, and before the people of Israel.

verses.esv: For I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.”
verses.kjv: For I will shew him how great things he must suffer for my name’s sake.
verses.bsb: I will show him how much he must suffer for My name.”

verses.esv: So Ananias departed and entered the house. And laying his hands on him he said, “Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus who appeared to you on the road by which you came has sent me so that you may regain your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
verses.kjv: And Ananias went his way, and entered into the house; and putting his hands on him said, Brother Saul, the Lord, even Jesus, that appeared unto thee in the way as thou camest, hath sent me, that thou mightest receive thy sight, and be filled with the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: So Ananias went to the house, and when he arrived, he placed his hands on Saul. “Brother Saul,” he said, “the Lord Jesus, who appeared to you on the road as you were coming here, has sent me so that you may see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”

verses.esv: And immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and he regained his sight. Then he rose and was baptized;
verses.kjv: And immediately there fell from his eyes as it had been scales: and he received sight forthwith, and arose, and was baptized.
verses.bsb: At that instant, something like scales fell from Saul’s eyes, and his sight was restored. He got up and was baptized,

verses.esv: and taking food, he was strengthened. For some days he was with the disciples at Damascus.
verses.kjv: And when he had received meat, he was strengthened. Then was Saul certain days with the disciples which were at Damascus.
verses.bsb: and after taking some food, he regained his strength. And he spent several days with the disciples in Damascus.

verses.esv: And immediately he proclaimed Jesus in the synagogues, saying, “He is the Son of God.”
verses.kjv: And straightway he preached Christ in the synagogues, that he is the Son of God.
verses.bsb: Saul promptly began to proclaim Jesus in the synagogues, declaring, “He is the Son of God.”

verses.esv: And all who heard him were amazed and said, “Is not this the man who made havoc in Jerusalem of those who called upon this name? And has he not come here for this purpose, to bring them bound before the chief priests?”
verses.kjv: But all that heard him were amazed, and said; Is not this he that destroyed them which called on this name in Jerusalem, and came hither for that intent, that he might bring them bound unto the chief priests?
verses.bsb: All who heard him were astounded and asked, “Isn’t this the man who wreaked havoc in Jerusalem on those who call on this name? And hasn’t he come here to take them as prisoners to the chief priests?”

verses.esv: But Saul increased all the more in strength, and confounded the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus was the Christ.
verses.kjv: But Saul increased the more in strength, and confounded the Jews which dwelt at Damascus, proving that this is very Christ.
verses.bsb: But Saul was empowered all the more, and he confounded the Jews living in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ.

verses.esv: When many days had passed, the Jews plotted to kill him,
verses.kjv: ¶ And after that many days were fulfilled, the Jews took counsel to kill him:
verses.bsb: After many days had passed, the Jews conspired to kill him,

verses.esv: but their plot became known to Saul. They were watching the gates day and night in order to kill him,
verses.kjv: But their laying await was known of Saul. And they watched the gates day and night to kill him.
verses.bsb: but Saul learned of their plot. Day and night they watched the city gates in order to kill him.

verses.esv: but his disciples took him by night and let him down through an opening in the wall, lowering him in a basket.
verses.kjv: Then the disciples took him by night, and let him down by the wall in a basket.
verses.bsb: One night, however, his disciples took him and lowered him in a basket through a window in the wall.

verses.esv: And when he had come to Jerusalem, he attempted to join the disciples. And they were all afraid of him, for they did not believe that he was a disciple.
verses.kjv: And when Saul was come to Jerusalem, he assayed to join himself to the disciples: but they were all afraid of him, and believed not that he was a disciple.
verses.bsb: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him, not believing that he was a disciple.

verses.esv: But Barnabas took him and brought him to the apostles and declared to them how on the road he had seen the Lord, who spoke to him, and how at Damascus he had preached boldly in the name of Jesus.
verses.kjv: But Barnabas took him, and brought him to the apostles, and declared unto them how he had seen the Lord in the way, and that he had spoken to him, and how he had preached boldly at Damascus in the name of Jesus.
verses.bsb: Then Barnabas brought him to the apostles and described how Saul had seen the Lord, who spoke to him on the road to Damascus, and how Saul had spoken boldly in that city in the name of Jesus.

verses.esv: So he went in and out among them at Jerusalem, preaching boldly in the name of the Lord.
verses.kjv: And he was with them coming in and going out at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: So Saul stayed with them, moving about freely in Jerusalem and speaking boldly in the name of the Lord.

verses.esv: And he spoke and disputed against the Hellenists. But they were seeking to kill him.
verses.kjv: And he spake boldly in the name of the Lord Jesus, and disputed against the Grecians: but they went about to slay him.
verses.bsb: He talked and debated with the Grecian Jews, but they tried to kill him.

verses.esv: And when the brothers learned this, they brought him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.
verses.kjv: Which when the brethren knew, they brought him down to Cæsarea, and sent him forth to Tarsus.
verses.bsb: When the brothers learned of this, they took him down to Caesarea and sent him off to Tarsus.

verses.esv: So the church throughout all Judea and Galilee and Samaria had peace and was being built up. And walking in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit, it multiplied.
verses.kjv: Then had the churches rest throughout all Judæa and Galilee and Samaria, and were edified; and walking in the fear of the Lord, and in the comfort of the Holy Ghost, were multiplied.
verses.bsb: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria experienced a time of peace. It grew in strength and numbers, living in the fear of the Lord and the encouragement of the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: Now as Peter went here and there among them all, he came down also to the saints who lived at Lydda.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as Peter passed throughout all quarters, he came down also to the saints which dwelt at Lydda.
verses.bsb: As Peter traveled throughout the area, he went to visit the saints in Lydda.

verses.esv: There he found a man named Aeneas, bedridden for eight years, who was paralyzed.
verses.kjv: And there he found a certain man named Æneas, which had kept his bed eight years, and was sick of the palsy.
verses.bsb: There he found a man named Aeneas, who had been paralyzed and bedridden for eight years.

verses.esv: And Peter said to him, “Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you; rise and make your bed.” And immediately he rose.
verses.kjv: And Peter said unto him, Æneas, Jesus Christ maketh thee whole: arise, and make thy bed. And he arose immediately.
verses.bsb: “Aeneas,” Peter said to him, “Jesus Christ heals you! Get up and put away your mat.” Immediately Aeneas got up,

verses.esv: And all the residents of Lydda and Sharon saw him, and they turned to the Lord.
verses.kjv: And all that dwelt at Lydda and Saron saw him, and turned to the Lord.
verses.bsb: and all who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.

verses.esv: Now there was in Joppa a disciple named Tabitha, which, translated, means Dorcas. She was full of good works and acts of charity.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now there was at Joppa a certain disciple named Tabitha, which by interpretation is called Dorcas: this woman was full of good works and almsdeeds which she did.
verses.bsb: In Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha (which is translated as Dorcas), who was always occupied with works of kindness and charity.

verses.esv: In those days she became ill and died, and when they had washed her, they laid her in an upper room.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that she was sick, and died: whom when they had washed, they laid her in an upper chamber.
verses.bsb: At that time, however, she became sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upper room.

verses.esv: Since Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples, hearing that Peter was there, sent two men to him, urging him, “Please come to us without delay.”
verses.kjv: And forasmuch as Lydda was nigh to Joppa, and the disciples had heard that Peter was there, they sent unto him two men, desiring him that he would not delay to come to them.
verses.bsb: Since Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples heard that Peter was there and sent two men to urge him, “Come to us without delay.”

verses.esv: So Peter rose and went with them. And when he arrived, they took him to the upper room. All the widows stood beside him weeping and showing tunics and other garments that Dorcas made while she was with them.
verses.kjv: Then Peter arose and went with them. When he was come, they brought him into the upper chamber: and all the widows stood by him weeping, and shewing the coats and garments which Dorcas made, while she was with them.
verses.bsb: So Peter got up and went with them. On his arrival, they took him to the upper room. All the widows stood around him, weeping and showing him the tunics and other clothing that Dorcas had made while she was still with them.

verses.esv: But Peter put them all outside, and knelt down and prayed; and turning to the body he said, “Tabitha, arise.” And she opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter she sat up.
verses.kjv: But Peter put them all forth, and kneeled down, and prayed; and turning him to the body said, Tabitha, arise. And she opened her eyes: and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
verses.bsb: Then Peter sent them all out of the room. He knelt down and prayed, and turning toward her body, he said, “Tabitha, get up!” She opened her eyes, and seeing Peter, she sat up.

verses.esv: And he gave her his hand and raised her up. Then, calling the saints and widows, he presented her alive.
verses.kjv: And he gave her his hand, and lifted her up, and when he had called the saints and widows, presented her alive.
verses.bsb: Peter took her by the hand and helped her up. Then he called the saints and widows and presented her to them alive.

verses.esv: And it became known throughout all Joppa, and many believed in the Lord.
verses.kjv: And it was known throughout all Joppa; and many believed in the Lord.
verses.bsb: This became known all over Joppa, and many people believed in the Lord.

verses.esv: And he stayed in Joppa for many days with one Simon, a tanner.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that he tarried many days in Joppa with one Simon a tanner.
verses.bsb: And Peter stayed for several days in Joppa with a tanner named Simon.

verses.esv: At Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion of what was known as the Italian Cohort,
verses.kjv: THERE was a certain man in Cæsarea called Cornelius, a centurion of the band called the Italian band,
verses.bsb: At Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion in what was called the Italian Regiment.

verses.esv: a devout man who feared God with all his household, gave alms generously to the people, and prayed continually to God.
verses.kjv: A devout man, and one that feared God with all his house, which gave much alms to the people, and prayed to God alway.
verses.bsb: He and all his household were devout and God-fearing. He gave generously to the people and prayed to God regularly.

verses.esv: About the ninth hour of the day he saw clearly in a vision an angel of God come in and say to him, “Cornelius.”
verses.kjv: He saw in a vision evidently about the ninth hour of the day an angel of God coming in to him, and saying unto him, Cornelius.
verses.bsb: One day at about the ninth hour, he had a clear vision of an angel of God who came to him and said, “Cornelius!”

verses.esv: And he stared at him in terror and said, “What is it, Lord?” And he said to him, “Your prayers and your alms have ascended as a memorial before God.
verses.kjv: And when he looked on him, he was afraid, and said, What is it, Lord? And he said unto him, Thy prayers and thine alms are come up for a memorial before God.
verses.bsb: Cornelius stared at him in fear and asked, “What is it, Lord?”  The angel answered, “Your prayers and gifts to the poor have ascended as a memorial offering before God.

verses.esv: And now send men to Joppa and bring one Simon who is called Peter.
verses.kjv: And now send men to Joppa, and call for one Simon, whose surname is Peter:
verses.bsb: Now send men to Joppa to call for a man named Simon who is called Peter.

verses.esv: He is lodging with one Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the sea.”
verses.kjv: He lodgeth with one Simon a tanner, whose house is by the sea side: he shall tell thee what thou oughtest to do.
verses.bsb: He is staying with Simon the tanner, whose house is by the sea.”

verses.esv: When the angel who spoke to him had departed, he called two of his servants and a devout soldier from among those who attended him,
verses.kjv: And when the angel which spake unto Cornelius was departed, he called two of his household servants, and a devout soldier of them that waited on him continually;
verses.bsb: When the angel who spoke to him had gone, Cornelius called two of his servants and a devout soldier from among his attendants.

verses.esv: and having related everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
verses.kjv: And when he had declared all these things unto them, he sent them to Joppa.
verses.bsb: He explained what had happened and sent them to Joppa.

verses.esv: The next day, as they were on their journey and approaching the city, Peter went up on the housetop about the sixth hour to pray.
verses.kjv: ¶ On the morrow, as they went on their journey, and drew nigh unto the city, Peter went up upon the housetop to pray about the sixth hour:
verses.bsb: The next day at about the sixth hour, as the men were approaching the city on their journey, Peter went up on the roof to pray.

verses.esv: And he became hungry and wanted something to eat, but while they were preparing it, he fell into a trance
verses.kjv: And he became very hungry, and would have eaten: but while they made ready, he fell into a trance,
verses.bsb: He became hungry and wanted something to eat, but while the meal was being prepared, he fell into a trance.

verses.esv: and saw the heavens opened and something like a great sheet descending, being let down by its four corners upon the earth.
verses.kjv: And saw heaven opened, and a certain vessel descending unto him, as it had been a great sheet knit at the four corners, and let down to the earth:
verses.bsb: He saw heaven open and something like a large sheet being let down to earth by its four corners.

verses.esv: In it were all kinds of animals and reptiles and birds of the air.
verses.kjv: Wherein were all manner of fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
verses.bsb: It contained all kinds of four-footed animals and reptiles of the earth, as well as birds of the air.

verses.esv: And there came a voice to him: “Rise, Peter; kill and eat.”
verses.kjv: And there came a voice to him, Rise, Peter; kill, and eat.
verses.bsb: Then a voice said to him: “Get up, Peter, kill and eat!”

verses.esv: But Peter said, “By no means, Lord; for I have never eaten anything that is common or unclean.”
verses.kjv: But Peter said, Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten any thing that is common or unclean.
verses.bsb: “No, Lord!” Peter answered. “I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.”

verses.esv: And the voice came to him again a second time, “What God has made clean, do not call common.”
verses.kjv: And the voice spake unto him again the second time, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.
verses.bsb: The voice spoke to him a second time: “Do not call anything impure that God has made clean.”

verses.esv: This happened three times, and the thing was taken up at once to heaven.
verses.kjv: This was done thrice: and the vessel was received up again into heaven.
verses.bsb: This happened three times, and all at once the sheet was taken back up into heaven.

verses.esv: Now while Peter was inwardly perplexed as to what the vision that he had seen might mean, behold, the men who were sent by Cornelius, having made inquiry for Simon's house, stood at the gate
verses.kjv: Now while Peter doubted in himself what this vision which he had seen should mean, behold, the men which were sent from Cornelius had made inquiry for Simon’s house, and stood before the gate,
verses.bsb: While Peter was puzzling over the meaning of the vision, the men sent by Cornelius found Simon’s house and approached the gate.

verses.esv: and called out to ask whether Simon who was called Peter was lodging there.
verses.kjv: And called, and asked whether Simon, which was surnamed Peter, were lodged there.
verses.bsb: They called out to ask if Simon called Peter was staying there.

verses.esv: And while Peter was pondering the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Behold, three men are looking for you.
verses.kjv: ¶ While Peter thought on the vision, the Spirit said unto him, Behold, three men seek thee.
verses.bsb: As Peter continued to reflect on the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Behold, three men are looking for you.

verses.esv: Rise and go down and accompany them without hesitation, for I have sent them.”
verses.kjv: Arise therefore, and get thee down, and go with them, doubting nothing: for I have sent them.
verses.bsb: So get up! Go downstairs and accompany them without hesitation, because I have sent them.”

verses.esv: And Peter went down to the men and said, “I am the one you are looking for. What is the reason for your coming?”
verses.kjv: Then Peter went down to the men which were sent unto him from Cornelius; and said, Behold, I am he whom ye seek: what is the cause wherefore ye are come?
verses.bsb: So Peter went down to the men and said, “Here am I, the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”

verses.esv: And they said, “Cornelius, a centurion, an upright and God-fearing man, who is well spoken of by the whole Jewish nation, was directed by a holy angel to send for you to come to his house and to hear what you have to say.”
verses.kjv: And they said, Cornelius the centurion, a just man, and one that feareth God, and of good report among all the nation of the Jews, was warned from God by an holy angel to send for thee into his house, and to hear words of thee.
verses.bsb: “Cornelius the centurion has sent us,” they said. “He is a righteous and God-fearing man with a good reputation among the whole Jewish nation. A holy angel instructed him to request your presence in his home so he could hear a message from you.”

verses.esv: So he invited them in to be his guests. The next day he rose and went away with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa accompanied him.
verses.kjv: Then called he them in, and lodged them. And on the morrow Peter went away with them, and certain brethren from Joppa accompanied him.
verses.bsb: So Peter invited them in as his guests. And the next day he got ready and went with them, accompanied by some of the brothers from Joppa.

verses.esv: And on the following day they entered Caesarea. Cornelius was expecting them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
verses.kjv: And the morrow after they entered into Cæsarea. And Cornelius waited for them, and had called together his kinsmen and near friends.
verses.bsb: The following day he arrived in Caesarea, where Cornelius was expecting them and had called together his relatives and close friends.

verses.esv: When Peter entered, Cornelius met him and fell down at his feet and worshiped him.
verses.kjv: And as Peter was coming in, Cornelius met him, and fell down at his feet, and worshipped him.
verses.bsb: As Peter was about to enter, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet to worship him.

verses.esv: But Peter lifted him up, saying, “Stand up; I too am a man.”
verses.kjv: But Peter took him up, saying, Stand up; I myself also am a man.
verses.bsb: But Peter helped him up. “Stand up,” he said, “I am only a man myself.”

verses.esv: And as he talked with him, he went in and found many persons gathered.
verses.kjv: And as he talked with him, he went in, and found many that were come together.
verses.bsb: As Peter talked with him, he went inside and found many people gathered together.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “You yourselves know how unlawful it is for a Jew to associate with or to visit anyone of another nation, but God has shown me that I should not call any person common or unclean.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Ye know how that it is an unlawful thing for a man that is a Jew to keep company, or come unto one of another nation; but God hath shewed me that I should not call any man common or unclean.
verses.bsb: He said to them, “You know how unlawful it is for a Jew to associate with a foreigner or visit him. But God has shown me that I should not call any man impure or unclean.

verses.esv: So when I was sent for, I came without objection. I ask then why you sent for me.”
verses.kjv: Therefore came I unto you without gainsaying, as soon as I was sent for: I ask therefore for what intent ye have sent for me?
verses.bsb: So when I was invited, I came without objection. I ask, then, why have you sent for me?”

verses.esv: And Cornelius said, “Four days ago, about this hour, I was praying in my house at the ninth hour, and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing
verses.kjv: And Cornelius said, Four days ago I was fasting until this hour; and at the ninth hour I prayed in my house, and, behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,
verses.bsb: Cornelius answered: “Four days ago I was in my house praying at this, the ninth hour. Suddenly a man in radiant clothing stood before me

verses.esv: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your alms have been remembered before God.
verses.kjv: And said, Cornelius, thy prayer is heard, and thine alms are had in remembrance in the sight of God.
verses.bsb: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard, and your gifts to the poor have been remembered before God.

verses.esv: Send therefore to Joppa and ask for Simon who is called Peter. He is lodging in the house of Simon, a tanner, by the sea.’
verses.kjv: Send therefore to Joppa, and call hither Simon, whose surname is Peter; he is lodged in the house of one Simon a tanner by the sea side: who, when he cometh, shall speak unto thee.
verses.bsb: Therefore send to Joppa for Simon, who is called Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, by the sea.’

verses.esv: So I sent for you at once, and you have been kind enough to come. Now therefore we are all here in the presence of God to hear all that you have been commanded by the Lord.”
verses.kjv: Immediately therefore I sent to thee; and thou hast well done that thou art come. Now therefore are we all here present before God, to hear all things that are commanded thee of God.
verses.bsb: So I sent for you immediately, and you were kind enough to come. Now then, we are all here in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has instructed you to tell us.”

verses.esv: So Peter opened his mouth and said: “Truly I understand that God shows no partiality,
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Peter opened his mouth, and said, Of a truth I perceive that God is no respecter of persons:
verses.bsb: Then Peter began to speak: “I now truly understand that God does not show favoritism,

verses.esv: but in every nation anyone who fears him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
verses.kjv: But in every nation he that feareth him, and worketh righteousness, is accepted with him.
verses.bsb: but welcomes those from every nation who fear Him and do what is right.

verses.esv: As for the word that he sent to Israel, preaching good news of peace through Jesus Christ (he is Lord of all),
verses.kjv: The word which God sent unto the children of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ: (he is Lord of all:)
verses.bsb: He has sent this message to the people of Israel, proclaiming the gospel of peace through Jesus Christ, who is Lord of all.

verses.esv: you yourselves know what happened throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee after the baptism that John proclaimed:
verses.kjv: That word, I say, ye know, which was published throughout all Judæa, and began from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;
verses.bsb: You yourselves know what has happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee with the baptism that John proclaimed:

verses.esv: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power. He went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.
verses.kjv: How God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Ghost and with power: who went about doing good, and healing all that were oppressed of the devil; for God was with him.
verses.bsb: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power, and how Jesus went around doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, because God was with Him.

verses.esv: And we are witnesses of all that he did both in the country of the Jews and in Jerusalem. They put him to death by hanging him on a tree,
verses.kjv: And we are witnesses of all things which he did both in the land of the Jews, and in Jerusalem; whom they slew and hanged on a tree:
verses.bsb: We are witnesses of all that He did, both in the land of the Jews and in Jerusalem. And although they put Him to death by hanging Him on a tree,

verses.esv: but God raised him on the third day and made him to appear,
verses.kjv: Him God raised up the third day, and shewed him openly;
verses.bsb: God raised Him up on the third day and caused Him to be seen—

verses.esv: not to all the people but to us who had been chosen by God as witnesses, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
verses.kjv: Not to all the people, but unto witnesses chosen before of God, even to us, who did eat and drink with him after he rose from the dead.
verses.bsb: not by all the people, but by the witnesses God had chosen beforehand, by us who ate and drank with Him after He rose from the dead.

verses.esv: And he commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one appointed by God to be judge of the living and the dead.
verses.kjv: And he commanded us to preach unto the people, and to testify that it is he which was ordained of God to be the Judge of quick and dead.
verses.bsb: And He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that He is the One appointed by God to judge the living and the dead.

verses.esv: To him all the prophets bear witness that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.”
verses.kjv: To him give all the prophets witness, that through his name whosoever believeth in him shall receive remission of sins.
verses.bsb: All the prophets testify about Him that everyone who believes in Him receives forgiveness of sins through His name.”

verses.esv: While Peter was still saying these things, the Holy Spirit fell on all who heard the word.
verses.kjv: ¶ While Peter yet spake these words, the Holy Ghost fell on all them which heard the word.
verses.bsb: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell upon all who heard his message.

verses.esv: And the believers from among the circumcised who had come with Peter were amazed, because the gift of the Holy Spirit was poured out even on the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: And they of the circumcision which believed were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because that on the Gentiles also was poured out the gift of the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: All the circumcised believers who had accompanied Peter were astounded that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.

verses.esv: For they were hearing them speaking in tongues and extolling God. Then Peter declared,
verses.kjv: For they heard them speak with tongues, and magnify God. Then answered Peter,
verses.bsb: For they heard them speaking in tongues and exalting God.  Then Peter said,

verses.esv: “Can anyone withhold water for baptizing these people, who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?”
verses.kjv: Can any man forbid water, that these should not be baptized, which have received the Holy Ghost as well as we?
verses.bsb: “Can anyone withhold the water to baptize these people? They have received the Holy Spirit just as we have!”

verses.esv: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to remain for some days.
verses.kjv: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of the Lord. Then prayed they him to tarry certain days.
verses.bsb: So he ordered that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay for a few days.

verses.esv: Now the apostles and the brothers who were throughout Judea heard that the Gentiles also had received the word of God.
verses.kjv: AND the apostles and brethren that were in Judæa heard that the Gentiles had also received the word of God.
verses.bsb: The apostles and brothers throughout Judea soon heard that the Gentiles also had received the word of God.

verses.esv: So when Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcision party criticized him, saying,
verses.kjv: And when Peter was come up to Jerusalem, they that were of the circumcision contended with him,
verses.bsb: So when Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers took issue with him

verses.esv: “You went to uncircumcised men and ate with them.”
verses.kjv: Saying, Thou wentest in to men uncircumcised, and didst eat with them.
verses.bsb: and said, “You visited uncircumcised men and ate with them.”

verses.esv: But Peter began and explained it to them in order:
verses.kjv: But Peter rehearsed the matter from the beginning, and expounded it by order unto them, saying,
verses.bsb: But Peter began and explained to them the whole sequence of events:

verses.esv: “I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision, something like a great sheet descending, being let down from heaven by its four corners, and it came down to me.
verses.kjv: I was in the city of Joppa praying: and in a trance I saw a vision, A certain vessel descend, as it had been a great sheet, let down from heaven by four corners; and it came even to me:
verses.bsb: “I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision of something like a large sheet being let down from heaven by its four corners, and it came right down to me.

verses.esv: Looking at it closely, I observed animals and beasts of prey and reptiles and birds of the air.
verses.kjv: Upon the which when I had fastened mine eyes, I considered, and saw fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
verses.bsb: I looked at it closely and saw four-footed animals of the earth, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds of the air.

verses.esv: And I heard a voice saying to me, ‘Rise, Peter; kill and eat.’
verses.kjv: And I heard a voice saying unto me, Arise, Peter; slay and eat.
verses.bsb: Then I heard a voice saying to me, ‘Get up, Peter, kill and eat.’

verses.esv: But I said, ‘By no means, Lord; for nothing common or unclean has ever entered my mouth.’
verses.kjv: But I said, Not so, Lord: for nothing common or unclean hath at any time entered into my mouth.
verses.bsb: ‘No, Lord,’ I said, ‘for nothing impure or unclean has ever entered my mouth.’

verses.esv: But the voice answered a second time from heaven, ‘What God has made clean, do not call common.’
verses.kjv: But the voice answered me again from heaven, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.
verses.bsb: But the voice spoke from heaven a second time, ‘Do not call anything impure that God has made clean.’

verses.esv: This happened three times, and all was drawn up again into heaven.
verses.kjv: And this was done three times: and all were drawn up again into heaven.
verses.bsb: This happened three times, and everything was drawn back up into heaven.

verses.esv: And behold, at that very moment three men arrived at the house in which we were, sent to me from Caesarea.
verses.kjv: And, behold, immediately there were three men already come unto the house where I was, sent from Cæsarea unto me.
verses.bsb: Just then three men sent to me from Caesarea stopped at the house where I was staying.

verses.esv: And the Spirit told me to go with them, making no distinction. These six brothers also accompanied me, and we entered the man's house.
verses.kjv: And the spirit bade me go with them, nothing doubting. Moreover these six brethren accompanied me, and we entered into the man’s house:
verses.bsb: The Spirit told me to accompany them without hesitation. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered the man’s home.

verses.esv: And he told us how he had seen the angel stand in his house and say, ‘Send to Joppa and bring Simon who is called Peter;
verses.kjv: And he shewed us how he had seen an angel in his house, which stood and said unto him, Send men to Joppa, and call for Simon, whose surname is Peter;
verses.bsb: He told us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, ‘Send to Joppa for Simon who is called Peter.

verses.esv: he will declare to you a message by which you will be saved, you and all your household.’
verses.kjv: Who shall tell thee words, whereby thou and all thy house shall be saved.
verses.bsb: He will convey to you a message by which you and all your household will be saved.’

verses.esv: As I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them just as on us at the beginning.
verses.kjv: And as I began to speak, the Holy Ghost fell on them, as on us at the beginning.
verses.bsb: As I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell upon them, just as He had fallen upon us at the beginning.

verses.esv: And I remembered the word of the Lord, how he said, ‘John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.’
verses.kjv: Then remembered I the word of the Lord, how that he said, John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: Then I remembered the word of the Lord, as He used to say, ‘John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.’

verses.esv: If then God gave the same gift to them as he gave to us when we believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I that I could stand in God's way?”
verses.kjv: Forasmuch then as God gave them the like gift as he did unto us, who believed on the Lord Jesus Christ; what was I, that I could withstand God?
verses.bsb: So if God gave them the same gift He gave us who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I to hinder the work of God?”

verses.esv: When they heard these things they fell silent. And they glorified God, saying, “Then to the Gentiles also God has granted repentance that leads to life.”
verses.kjv: When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, Then hath God also to the Gentiles granted repentance unto life.
verses.bsb: When they heard this, their objections were put to rest, and they glorified God, saying, “So then, God has granted even the Gentiles repentance unto life.”

verses.esv: Now those who were scattered because of the persecution that arose over Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia and Cyprus and Antioch, speaking the word to no one except Jews.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now they which were scattered abroad upon the persecution that arose about Stephen travelled as far as Phenice, and Cyprus, and Antioch, preaching the word to none but unto the Jews only.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile those scattered by the persecution that began with Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, speaking the message only to Jews.

verses.esv: But there were some of them, men of Cyprus and Cyrene, who on coming to Antioch spoke to the Hellenists also, preaching the Lord Jesus.
verses.kjv: And some of them were men of Cyprus and Cyrene, which, when they were come to Antioch, spake unto the Grecians, preaching the Lord Jesus.
verses.bsb: But some of them, men from Cyprus and Cyrene, went to Antioch and began speaking to the Greeks as well, proclaiming the good news about the Lord Jesus.
