verses.esv: And the hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number who believed turned to the Lord.
verses.kjv: And the hand of the Lord was with them: and a great number believed, and turned unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: The hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number of people believed and turned to the Lord.

verses.esv: The report of this came to the ears of the church in Jerusalem, and they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then tidings of these things came unto the ears of the church which was in Jerusalem: and they sent forth Barnabas, that he should go as far as Antioch.
verses.bsb: When news of this reached the ears of the church in Jerusalem, they sent Barnabas to Antioch.

verses.esv: When he came and saw the grace of God, he was glad, and he exhorted them all to remain faithful to the Lord with steadfast purpose,
verses.kjv: Who, when he came, and had seen the grace of God, was glad, and exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they would cleave unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: When he arrived and saw the grace of God, he rejoiced and encouraged them all to abide in the Lord with all their hearts.

verses.esv: for he was a good man, full of the Holy Spirit and of faith. And a great many people were added to the Lord.
verses.kjv: For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Ghost and of faith: and much people was added unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: Barnabas was a good man, full of the Holy Spirit and faith, and a great number of people were brought to the Lord.

verses.esv: So Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul,
verses.kjv: Then departed Barnabas to Tarsus, for to seek Saul:
verses.bsb: Then Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul,

verses.esv: and when he had found him, he brought him to Antioch. For a whole year they met with the church and taught a great many people. And in Antioch the disciples were first called Christians.
verses.kjv: And when he had found him, he brought him unto Antioch. And it came to pass, that a whole year they assembled themselves with the church, and taught much people. And the disciples were called Christians first in Antioch.
verses.bsb: and when he found him, he brought him back to Antioch. So for a full year they met together with the church and taught large numbers of people. The disciples were first called Christians at Antioch.

verses.esv: Now in these days prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
verses.kjv: ¶ And in these days came prophets from Jerusalem unto Antioch.
verses.bsb: In those days some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.

verses.esv: And one of them named Agabus stood up and foretold by the Spirit that there would be a great famine over all the world (this took place in the days of Claudius).
verses.kjv: And there stood up one of them named Agabus, and signified by the spirit that there should be great dearth throughout all the world: which came to pass in the days of Claudius Cæsar.
verses.bsb: One of them named Agabus stood up and predicted through the Spirit that a great famine would sweep across the whole world. (This happened under Claudius.)

verses.esv: So the disciples determined, every one according to his ability, to send relief to the brothers living in Judea.
verses.kjv: Then the disciples, every man according to his ability, determined to send relief unto the brethren which dwelt in Judæa:
verses.bsb: So the disciples, each according to his ability, decided to send relief to the brothers living in Judea.

verses.esv: And they did so, sending it to the elders by the hand of Barnabas and Saul.
verses.kjv: Which also they did, and sent it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.
verses.bsb: This they did, sending their gifts to the elders with Barnabas and Saul.

verses.esv: About that time Herod the king laid violent hands on some who belonged to the church.
verses.kjv: NOW about that time Herod the king stretched forth his hands to vex certain of the church.
verses.bsb: About that time, King Herod reached out to harm some who belonged to the church.

verses.esv: He killed James the brother of John with the sword,
verses.kjv: And he killed James the brother of John with the sword.
verses.bsb: He had James, the brother of John, put to death with the sword.

verses.esv: and when he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also. This was during the days of Unleavened Bread.
verses.kjv: And because he saw it pleased the Jews, he proceeded further to take Peter also. (Then were the days of unleavened bread.)
verses.bsb: And seeing that this pleased the Jews, Herod proceeded to seize Peter during the Feast of Unleavened Bread.

verses.esv: And when he had seized him, he put him in prison, delivering him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending after the Passover to bring him out to the people.
verses.kjv: And when he had apprehended him, he put him in prison, and delivered him to four quaternions of soldiers to keep him; intending after Easter to bring him forth to the people.
verses.bsb: He arrested him and put him in prison, handing him over to be guarded by four squads of four soldiers each. Herod intended to bring him out to the people after the Passover.

verses.esv: So Peter was kept in prison, but earnest prayer for him was made to God by the church.
verses.kjv: Peter therefore was kept in prison: but prayer was made without ceasing of the church unto God for him.
verses.bsb: So Peter was kept in prison, but the church was fervently praying to God for him.

verses.esv: Now when Herod was about to bring him out, on that very night, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and sentries before the door were guarding the prison.
verses.kjv: And when Herod would have brought him forth, the same night Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains: and the keepers before the door kept the prison.
verses.bsb: On the night before Herod was to bring him to trial, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, with sentries standing guard at the entrance to the prison.

verses.esv: And behold, an angel of the Lord stood next to him, and a light shone in the cell. He struck Peter on the side and woke him, saying, “Get up quickly.” And the chains fell off his hands.
verses.kjv: And, behold, the angel of the Lord came upon him, and a light shined in the prison: and he smote Peter on the side, and raised him up, saying, Arise up quickly. And his chains fell off from his hands.
verses.bsb: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared and a light shone in the cell. He tapped Peter on the side and woke him up, saying, “Get up quickly.” And the chains fell off his wrists.

verses.esv: And the angel said to him, “Dress yourself and put on your sandals.” And he did so. And he said to him, “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.”
verses.kjv: And the angel said unto him, Gird thyself, and bind on thy sandals. And so he did. And he saith unto him, Cast thy garment about thee, and follow me.
verses.bsb: “Get dressed and put on your sandals,” said the angel. Peter did so, and the angel told him, “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me.”

verses.esv: And he went out and followed him. He did not know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
verses.kjv: And he went out, and followed him; and wist not that it was true which was done by the angel; but thought he saw a vision.
verses.bsb: So Peter followed him out, but he was unaware that what the angel was doing was real. He thought he was only seeing a vision.

verses.esv: When they had passed the first and the second guard, they came to the iron gate leading into the city. It opened for them of its own accord, and they went out and went along one street, and immediately the angel left him.
verses.kjv: When they were past the first and the second ward, they came unto the iron gate that leadeth unto the city; which opened to them of his own accord: and they went out, and passed on through one street; and forthwith the angel departed from him.
verses.bsb: They passed the first and second guards and came to the iron gate leading to the city, which opened for them by itself. When they had gone outside and walked the length of one block, the angel suddenly left him.

verses.esv: When Peter came to himself, he said, “Now I am sure that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.”
verses.kjv: And when Peter was come to himself, he said, Now I know of a surety, that the Lord hath sent his angel, and hath delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from all the expectation of the people of the Jews.
verses.bsb: Then Peter came to himself and said, “Now I know for sure that the Lord has sent His angel and rescued me from Herod’s grasp and from everything the Jewish people were anticipating.”

verses.esv: When he realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose other name was Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.
verses.kjv: And when he had considered the thing, he came to the house of Mary the mother of John, whose surname was Mark; where many were gathered together praying.
verses.bsb: And when he had realized this, he went to the house of Mary the mother of John, also called Mark, where many people had gathered together and were praying.

verses.esv: And when he knocked at the door of the gateway, a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer.
verses.kjv: And as Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a damsel came to hearken, named Rhoda.
verses.bsb: He knocked at the outer gate, and a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer it.

verses.esv: Recognizing Peter's voice, in her joy she did not open the gate but ran in and reported that Peter was standing at the gate.
verses.kjv: And when she knew Peter’s voice, she opened not the gate for gladness, but ran in, and told how Peter stood before the gate.
verses.bsb: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed that she forgot to open the gate, but ran inside and announced, “Peter is standing at the gate!”

verses.esv: They said to her, “You are out of your mind.” But she kept insisting that it was so, and they kept saying, “It is his angel!”
verses.kjv: And they said unto her, Thou art mad. But she constantly affirmed that it was even so. Then said they, It is his angel.
verses.bsb: “You are out of your mind,” they told her. But when she kept insisting it was so, they said, “It must be his angel.”

verses.esv: But Peter continued knocking, and when they opened, they saw him and were amazed.
verses.kjv: But Peter continued knocking: and when they had opened the door, and saw him, they were astonished.
verses.bsb: But Peter kept on knocking, and when they opened the door and saw him, they were astounded.

verses.esv: But motioning to them with his hand to be silent, he described to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, “Tell these things to James and to the brothers.” Then he departed and went to another place.
verses.kjv: But he, beckoning unto them with the hand to hold their peace, declared unto them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, Go shew these things unto James, and to the brethren. And he departed, and went into another place.
verses.bsb: Peter motioned with his hand for silence, and he described how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. “Send word to James and to the brothers,” he said, and he left for another place.

verses.esv: Now when day came, there was no little disturbance among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
verses.kjv: Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers, what was become of Peter.
verses.bsb: At daybreak there was no small commotion among the soldiers as to what had become of Peter.

verses.esv: And after Herod searched for him and did not find him, he examined the sentries and ordered that they should be put to death. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and spent time there.
verses.kjv: And when Herod had sought for him, and found him not, he examined the keepers, and commanded that they should be put to death. And he went down from Judæa to Cæsarea, and there abode.
verses.bsb: After Herod had searched for him unsuccessfully, he examined the guards and ordered that they be executed. Then he went down from Judea to Caesarea and spent some time there.

verses.esv: Now Herod was angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon, and they came to him with one accord, and having persuaded Blastus, the king's chamberlain, they asked for peace, because their country depended on the king's country for food.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Herod was highly displeased with them of Tyre and Sidon: but they came with one accord to him, and, having made Blastus the king’s chamberlain their friend, desired peace; because their country was nourished by the king’s country.
verses.bsb: Now Herod was in a furious dispute with the people of Tyre and Sidon, and they convened before him. Having secured the support of Blastus, the king’s chamberlain, they asked for peace, because their region depended on the king’s country for food.

verses.esv: On an appointed day Herod put on his royal robes, took his seat upon the throne, and delivered an oration to them.
verses.kjv: And upon a set day Herod, arrayed in royal apparel, sat upon his throne, and made an oration unto them.
verses.bsb: On the appointed day, Herod donned his royal robes, sat on his throne, and addressed the people.

verses.esv: And the people were shouting, “The voice of a god, and not of a man!”
verses.kjv: And the people gave a shout, saying, It is the voice of a god, and not of a man.
verses.bsb: And they began to shout, “This is the voice of a god, not a man!”

verses.esv: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck him down, because he did not give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and breathed his last.
verses.kjv: And immediately the angel of the Lord smote him, because he gave not God the glory: and he was eaten of worms, and gave up the ghost.
verses.bsb: Immediately, because Herod did not give glory to God, an angel of the Lord struck him down, and he was eaten by worms and died.

verses.esv: But the word of God increased and multiplied.
verses.kjv: ¶ But the word of God grew and multiplied.
verses.bsb: But the word of God continued to spread and multiply.

verses.esv: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem when they had completed their service, bringing with them John, whose other name was Mark.
verses.kjv: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem, when they had fulfilled their ministry, and took with them John, whose surname was Mark.
verses.bsb: When Barnabas and Saul had fulfilled their mission to Jerusalem, they returned, bringing with them John, also called Mark.

verses.esv: Now there were in the church at Antioch prophets and teachers, Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen a lifelong friend of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
verses.kjv: NOW there were in the church that was at Antioch certain prophets and teachers; as Barnabas, and Simeon that was called Niger, and Lucius of Cyrene, and Manaen, which had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
verses.bsb: Now in the church at Antioch there were prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen (who had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch), and Saul.

verses.esv: While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
verses.kjv: As they ministered to the Lord, and fasted, the Holy Ghost said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work whereunto I have called them.
verses.bsb: While they were worshiping the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Set apart for Me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”

verses.esv: Then after fasting and praying they laid their hands on them and sent them off.
verses.kjv: And when they had fasted and prayed, and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.
verses.bsb: And after they had fasted and prayed, they laid their hands on them and sent them off.

verses.esv: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia, and from there they sailed to Cyprus.
verses.kjv: ¶ So they, being sent forth by the Holy Ghost, departed unto Seleucia; and from thence they sailed to Cyprus.
verses.bsb: So Barnabas and Saul, sent forth by the Holy Spirit, went down to Seleucia and sailed from there to Cyprus.

verses.esv: When they arrived at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews. And they had John to assist them.
verses.kjv: And when they were at Salamis, they preached the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews: and they had also John to their minister.
verses.bsb: When they arrived at Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the Jewish synagogues. And John was with them as their helper.

verses.esv: When they had gone through the whole island as far as Paphos, they came upon a certain magician, a Jewish false prophet named Bar-Jesus.
verses.kjv: And when they had gone through the isle unto Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar-jesus:
verses.bsb: They traveled through the whole island as far as Paphos, where they found a Jewish sorcerer and false prophet named Bar-Jesus,

verses.esv: He was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, a man of intelligence, who summoned Barnabas and Saul and sought to hear the word of God.
verses.kjv: Which was with the deputy of the country, Sergius Paulus, a prudent man; who called for Barnabas and Saul, and desired to hear the word of God.
verses.bsb: an attendant of the proconsul, Sergius Paulus. The proconsul, a man of intelligence, summoned Barnabas and Saul because he wanted to hear the word of God.

verses.esv: But Elymas the magician (for that is the meaning of his name) opposed them, seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith.
verses.kjv: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn away the deputy from the faith.
verses.bsb: But Elymas the sorcerer (for that is what his name means) opposed them and tried to turn the proconsul from the faith.

verses.esv: But Saul, who was also called Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, looked intently at him
verses.kjv: Then Saul, (who also is called Paul,) filled with the Holy Ghost, set his eyes on him,
verses.bsb: Then Saul, who was also called Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, looked directly at Elymas

verses.esv: and said, “You son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, full of all deceit and villainy, will you not stop making crooked the straight paths of the Lord?
verses.kjv: And said, O full of all subtilty and all mischief, thou child of the devil, thou enemy of all righteousness, wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?
verses.bsb: and said, “O child of the devil and enemy of all righteousness, you are full of all kinds of deceit and trickery! Will you never stop perverting the straight ways of the Lord?

verses.esv: And now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon you, and you will be blind and unable to see the sun for a time.” Immediately mist and darkness fell upon him, and he went about seeking people to lead him by the hand.
verses.kjv: And now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun for a season. And immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkness; and he went about seeking some to lead him by the hand.
verses.bsb: Now look, the hand of the Lord is against you, and for a time you will be blind and unable to see the light of the sun.” Immediately mist and darkness came over him, and he groped about, seeking someone to lead him by the hand.

verses.esv: Then the proconsul believed, when he saw what had occurred, for he was astonished at the teaching of the Lord.
verses.kjv: Then the deputy, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the doctrine of the Lord.
verses.bsb: When the proconsul saw what had happened, he believed, for he was astonished at the teaching about the Lord.

verses.esv: Now Paul and his companions set sail from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia. And John left them and returned to Jerusalem,
verses.kjv: Now when Paul and his company loosed from Paphos, they came to Perga in Pamphylia: and John departing from them returned to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: After setting sail from Paphos, Paul and his companions came to Perga in Pamphylia, where John left them to return to Jerusalem.

verses.esv: but they went on from Perga and came to Antioch in Pisidia. And on the Sabbath day they went into the synagogue and sat down.
verses.kjv: ¶ But when they departed from Perga, they came to Antioch in Pisidia, and went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and sat down.
verses.bsb: And from Perga, they traveled inland to Pisidian Antioch, where they entered the synagogue on the Sabbath and sat down.

verses.esv: After the reading from the Law and the Prophets, the rulers of the synagogue sent a message to them, saying, “Brothers, if you have any word of encouragement for the people, say it.”
verses.kjv: And after the reading of the law and the prophets the rulers of the synagogue sent unto them, saying, Ye men and brethren, if ye have any word of exhortation for the people, say on.
verses.bsb: After the reading from the Law and the Prophets, the synagogue leaders sent word to them: “Brothers, if you have a word of encouragement for the people, please speak.”

verses.esv: So Paul stood up, and motioning with his hand said: “Men of Israel and you who fear God, listen.
verses.kjv: Then Paul stood up, and beckoning with his hand said, Men of Israel, and ye that fear God, give audience.
verses.bsb: Paul stood up, motioned with his hand, and began to speak: “Men of Israel and you Gentiles who fear God, listen to me!

verses.esv: The God of this people Israel chose our fathers and made the people great during their stay in the land of Egypt, and with uplifted arm he led them out of it.
verses.kjv: The God of this people of Israel chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they dwelt as strangers in the land of Egypt, and with an high arm brought he them out of it.
verses.bsb: The God of the people of Israel chose our fathers. He made them into a great people during their stay in Egypt, and with an uplifted arm He led them out of that land.

verses.esv: And for about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.
verses.kjv: And about the time of forty years suffered he their manners in the wilderness.
verses.bsb: He endured their conduct for about forty years in the wilderness.

verses.esv: And after destroying seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land as an inheritance.
verses.kjv: And when he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Chanaan, he divided their land to them by lot.
verses.bsb: And having vanquished seven nations in Canaan, He gave their land to His people as an inheritance.

verses.esv: All this took about 450 years. And after that he gave them judges until Samuel the prophet.
verses.kjv: And after that he gave unto them judges about the space of four hundred and fifty years, until Samuel the prophet.
verses.bsb: All this took about 450 years.  After this, God gave them judges until the time of Samuel the prophet.

verses.esv: Then they asked for a king, and God gave them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.
verses.kjv: And afterward they desired a king: and God gave unto them Saul the son of Cis, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, by the space of forty years.
verses.bsb: Then the people asked for a king, and God gave them forty years under Saul son of Kish, from the tribe of Benjamin.

verses.esv: And when he had removed him, he raised up David to be their king, of whom he testified and said, ‘I have found in David the son of Jesse a man after my heart, who will do all my will.’
verses.kjv: And when he had removed him, he raised up unto them David to be their king; to whom also he gave testimony, and said, I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after mine own heart, which shall fulfil all my will.
verses.bsb: After removing Saul, He raised up David as their king and testified about him: ‘I have found David son of Jesse a man after My own heart; he will carry out My will in its entirety.’

verses.esv: Of this man's offspring God has brought to Israel a Savior, Jesus, as he promised.
verses.kjv: Of this man’s seed hath God according to his promise raised unto Israel a Saviour, Jesus:
verses.bsb: From the descendants of this man, God has brought to Israel the Savior Jesus, as He promised.

verses.esv: Before his coming, John had proclaimed a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
verses.kjv: When John had first preached before his coming the baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
verses.bsb: Before the arrival of Jesus, John preached a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.

verses.esv: And as John was finishing his course, he said, ‘What do you suppose that I am? I am not he. No, but behold, after me one is coming, the sandals of whose feet I am not worthy to untie.’
verses.kjv: And as John fulfilled his course, he said, Whom think ye that I am? I am not he. But, behold, there cometh one after me, whose shoes of his feet I am not worthy to loose.
verses.bsb: As John was completing his course, he said, ‘Who do you suppose I am? I am not that One. But He is coming after me whose sandals I am not worthy to untie.’

verses.esv: “Brothers, sons of the family of Abraham, and those among you who fear God, to us has been sent the message of this salvation.
verses.kjv: Men and brethren, children of the stock of Abraham, and whosoever among you feareth God, to you is the word of this salvation sent.
verses.bsb: Brothers, children of Abraham, and you Gentiles who fear God, it is to us that this message of salvation has been sent.

verses.esv: For those who live in Jerusalem and their rulers, because they did not recognize him nor understand the utterances of the prophets, which are read every Sabbath, fulfilled them by condemning him.
verses.kjv: For they that dwell at Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they knew him not, nor yet the voices of the prophets which are read every sabbath day, they have fulfilled them in condemning him.
verses.bsb: The people of Jerusalem and their rulers did not recognize Jesus, yet in condemning Him they fulfilled the words of the prophets that are read every Sabbath.

verses.esv: And though they found in him no guilt worthy of death, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
verses.kjv: And though they found no cause of death in him, yet desired they Pilate that he should be slain.
verses.bsb: And though they found no ground for a death sentence, they asked Pilate to have Him executed.

verses.esv: And when they had carried out all that was written of him, they took him down from the tree and laid him in a tomb.
verses.kjv: And when they had fulfilled all that was written of him, they took him down from the tree, and laid him in a sepulchre.
verses.bsb: When they had carried out all that was written about Him, they took Him down from the tree and laid Him in a tomb.

verses.esv: But God raised him from the dead,
verses.kjv: But God raised him from the dead:
verses.bsb: But God raised Him from the dead,

verses.esv: and for many days he appeared to those who had come up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are now his witnesses to the people.
verses.kjv: And he was seen many days of them which came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses unto the people.
verses.bsb: and for many days He was seen by those who had accompanied Him from Galilee to Jerusalem. They are now His witnesses to our people.

verses.esv: And we bring you the good news that what God promised to the fathers,
verses.kjv: And we declare unto you glad tidings, how that the promise which was made unto the fathers,
verses.bsb: And now we proclaim to you the good news: What God promised our fathers

verses.esv: this he has fulfilled to us their children by raising Jesus, as also it is written in the second Psalm, “‘You are my Son, today I have begotten you.’
verses.kjv: God hath fulfilled the same unto us their children, in that he hath raised up Jesus again; as it is also written in the second psalm, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee.
verses.bsb: He has fulfilled for us, their children, by raising up Jesus. As it is written in the second Psalm:  ‘You are My Son; today I have become Your Father.’

verses.esv: And as for the fact that he raised him from the dead, no more to return to corruption, he has spoken in this way, “‘I will give you the holy and sure blessings of David.’
verses.kjv: And as concerning that he raised him up from the dead, now no more to return to corruption, he said on this wise, I will give you the sure mercies of David.
verses.bsb: In fact, God raised Him from the dead, never to see decay. As He has said:  ‘I will give you the holy and sure blessings promised to David.’

verses.esv: Therefore he says also in another psalm, “‘You will not let your Holy One see corruption.’
verses.kjv: Wherefore he saith also in another psalm, Thou shalt not suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
verses.bsb: So also, He says in another Psalm:  ‘You will not let Your Holy One see decay.’

verses.esv: For David, after he had served the purpose of God in his own generation, fell asleep and was laid with his fathers and saw corruption,
verses.kjv: For David, after he had served his own generation by the will of God, fell on sleep, and was laid unto his fathers, and saw corruption:
verses.bsb: For when David had served God’s purpose in his own generation, he fell asleep. His body was buried with his fathers and saw decay.

verses.esv: but he whom God raised up did not see corruption.
verses.kjv: But he, whom God raised again, saw no corruption.
verses.bsb: But the One whom God raised from the dead did not see decay.

verses.esv: Let it be known to you therefore, brothers, that through this man forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you,
verses.kjv: ¶ Be it known unto you therefore, men and brethren, that through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins:
verses.bsb: Therefore let it be known to you, brothers, that through Jesus the forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you.

verses.esv: and by him everyone who believes is freed from everything from which you could not be freed by the law of Moses.
verses.kjv: And by him all that believe are justified from all things, from which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses.
verses.bsb: Through Him everyone who believes is justified from everything you could not be justified from by the law of Moses.

verses.esv: Beware, therefore, lest what is said in the Prophets should come about:
verses.kjv: Beware therefore, lest that come upon you, which is spoken of in the prophets;
verses.bsb: Watch out, then, that what was spoken by the prophets does not happen to you:

verses.esv: “‘Look, you scoffers, be astounded and perish; for I am doing a work in your days, a work that you will not believe, even if one tells it to you.’”
verses.kjv: Behold, ye despisers, and wonder, and perish: for I work a work in your days, a work which ye shall in no wise believe, though a man declare it unto you.
verses.bsb: ‘Look, you scoffers, wonder and perish! For I am doing a work in your days that you would never believe, even if someone told you.’”

verses.esv: As they went out, the people begged that these things might be told them the next Sabbath.
verses.kjv: And when the Jews were gone out of the synagogue, the Gentiles besought that these words might be preached to them the next sabbath.
verses.bsb: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people urged them to continue this message on the next Sabbath.

verses.esv: And after the meeting of the synagogue broke up, many Jews and devout converts to Judaism followed Paul and Barnabas, who, as they spoke with them, urged them to continue in the grace of God.
verses.kjv: Now when the congregation was broken up, many of the Jews and religious proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas: who, speaking to them, persuaded them to continue in the grace of God.
verses.bsb: After the synagogue was dismissed, many of the Jews and devout converts to Judaism followed Paul and Barnabas, who spoke to them and urged them to continue in the grace of God.

verses.esv: The next Sabbath almost the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the next sabbath day came almost the whole city together to hear the word of God.
verses.bsb: On the following Sabbath, nearly the whole city gathered to hear the word of the Lord.

verses.esv: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy and began to contradict what was spoken by Paul, reviling him.
verses.kjv: But when the Jews saw the multitudes, they were filled with envy, and spake against those things which were spoken by Paul, contradicting and blaspheming.
verses.bsb: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy, and they blasphemously contradicted what Paul was saying.

verses.esv: And Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, saying, “It was necessary that the word of God be spoken first to you. Since you thrust it aside and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, behold, we are turning to the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: Then Paul and Barnabas waxed bold, and said, It was necessary that the word of God should first have been spoken to you: but seeing ye put it from you, and judge yourselves unworthy of everlasting life, lo, we turn to the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: Then Paul and Barnabas answered them boldly: “It was necessary to speak the word of God to you first. But since you reject it and do not consider yourselves worthy of eternal life, we now turn to the Gentiles.

verses.esv: For so the Lord has commanded us, saying, “‘I have made you a light for the Gentiles, that you may bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’”
verses.kjv: For so hath the Lord commanded us, saying, I have set thee to be a light of the Gentiles, that thou shouldest be for salvation unto the ends of the earth.
verses.bsb: For this is what the Lord has commanded us:  ‘I have made you a light for the Gentiles, to bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’”

verses.esv: And when the Gentiles heard this, they began rejoicing and glorifying the word of the Lord, and as many as were appointed to eternal life believed.
verses.kjv: And when the Gentiles heard this, they were glad, and glorified the word of the Lord: and as many as were ordained to eternal life believed.
verses.bsb: When the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and glorified the word of the Lord, and all who were appointed for eternal life believed.

verses.esv: And the word of the Lord was spreading throughout the whole region.
verses.kjv: And the word of the Lord was published throughout all the region.
verses.bsb: And the word of the Lord spread throughout that region.

verses.esv: But the Jews incited the devout women of high standing and the leading men of the city, stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and drove them out of their district.
verses.kjv: But the Jews stirred up the devout and honourable women, and the chief men of the city, and raised persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and expelled them out of their coasts.
verses.bsb: The Jews, however, incited the religious women of prominence and the leading men of the city. They stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas and drove them out of their district.

verses.esv: But they shook off the dust from their feet against them and went to Iconium.
verses.kjv: But they shook off the dust of their feet against them, and came unto Iconium.
verses.bsb: So they shook the dust off their feet in protest against them and went to Iconium.

verses.esv: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: And the disciples were filled with joy, and with the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: Now at Iconium they entered together into the Jewish synagogue and spoke in such a way that a great number of both Jews and Greeks believed.
verses.kjv: AND it came to pass in Iconium, that they went both together into the synagogue of the Jews, and so spake, that a great multitude both of the Jews and also of the Greeks believed.
verses.bsb: At Iconium, Paul and Barnabas went as usual into the Jewish synagogue, where they spoke so well that a great number of Jews and Greeks believed.

verses.esv: But the unbelieving Jews stirred up the Gentiles and poisoned their minds against the brothers.
verses.kjv: But the unbelieving Jews stirred up the Gentiles, and made their minds evil affected against the brethren.
verses.bsb: But the unbelieving Jews stirred up the Gentiles and poisoned their minds against the brothers.

verses.esv: So they remained for a long time, speaking boldly for the Lord, who bore witness to the word of his grace, granting signs and wonders to be done by their hands.
verses.kjv: Long time therefore abode they speaking boldly in the Lord, which gave testimony unto the word of his grace, and granted signs and wonders to be done by their hands.
verses.bsb: So Paul and Barnabas spent considerable time there, speaking boldly for the Lord, who affirmed the message of His grace by enabling them to perform signs and wonders.

verses.esv: But the people of the city were divided; some sided with the Jews and some with the apostles.
verses.kjv: But the multitude of the city was divided: and part held with the Jews, and part with the apostles.
verses.bsb: The people of the city were divided. Some sided with the Jews, and others with the apostles.

verses.esv: When an attempt was made by both Gentiles and Jews, with their rulers, to mistreat them and to stone them,
verses.kjv: And when there was an assault made both of the Gentiles, and also of the Jews with their rulers, to use them despitefully, and to stone them,
verses.bsb: But when the Gentiles and Jews, together with their rulers, set out to mistreat and stone them,

verses.esv: they learned of it and fled to Lystra and Derbe, cities of Lycaonia, and to the surrounding country,
verses.kjv: They were ware of it, and fled unto Lystra and Derbe, cities of Lycaonia, and unto the region that lieth round about:
verses.bsb: they found out about it and fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and to the surrounding region,

verses.esv: and there they continued to preach the gospel.
verses.kjv: And there they preached the gospel.
verses.bsb: where they continued to preach the gospel.

verses.esv: Now at Lystra there was a man sitting who could not use his feet. He was crippled from birth and had never walked.
verses.kjv: ¶ And there sat a certain man at Lystra, impotent in his feet, being a cripple from his mother’s womb, who never had walked:
verses.bsb: In Lystra there sat a man crippled in his feet, who was lame from birth and had never walked.

verses.esv: He listened to Paul speaking. And Paul, looking intently at him and seeing that he had faith to be made well,
verses.kjv: The same heard Paul speak: who stedfastly beholding him, and perceiving that he had faith to be healed,
verses.bsb: This man was listening to the words of Paul, who looked intently at him and saw that he had faith to be healed.

verses.esv: said in a loud voice, “Stand upright on your feet.” And he sprang up and began walking.
verses.kjv: Said with a loud voice, Stand upright on thy feet. And he leaped and walked.
verses.bsb: In a loud voice Paul called out, “Stand up on your feet!” And the man jumped up and began to walk.

verses.esv: And when the crowds saw what Paul had done, they lifted up their voices, saying in Lycaonian, “The gods have come down to us in the likeness of men!”
verses.kjv: And when the people saw what Paul had done, they lifted up their voices, saying in the speech of Lycaonia, The gods are come down to us in the likeness of men.
verses.bsb: When the crowds saw what Paul had done, they lifted up their voices in the Lycaonian language: “The gods have come down to us in human form!”

verses.esv: Barnabas they called Zeus, and Paul, Hermes, because he was the chief speaker.
verses.kjv: And they called Barnabas, Jupiter; and Paul, Mercurius, because he was the chief speaker.
verses.bsb: Barnabas they called Zeus, and Paul they called Hermes, because he was the chief speaker.

verses.esv: And the priest of Zeus, whose temple was at the entrance to the city, brought oxen and garlands to the gates and wanted to offer sacrifice with the crowds.
verses.kjv: Then the priest of Jupiter, which was before their city, brought oxen and garlands unto the gates, and would have done sacrifice with the people.
verses.bsb: The priest of Zeus, whose temple was just outside the city, brought bulls and wreaths to the city gates, hoping to offer a sacrifice along with the crowds.

verses.esv: But when the apostles Barnabas and Paul heard of it, they tore their garments and rushed out into the crowd, crying out,
verses.kjv: Which when the apostles, Barnabas and Paul, heard of, they rent their clothes, and ran in among the people, crying out,
verses.bsb: But when the apostles Barnabas and Paul found out about this, they tore their clothes and rushed into the crowd, shouting,

verses.esv: “Men, why are you doing these things? We also are men, of like nature with you, and we bring you good news, that you should turn from these vain things to a living God, who made the heaven and the earth and the sea and all that is in them.
verses.kjv: And saying, Sirs, why do ye these things? We also are men of like passions with you, and preach unto you that ye should turn from these vanities unto the living God, which made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all things that are therein:
verses.bsb: “Men, why are you doing this? We too are only men, human like you. We are bringing you good news that you should turn from these worthless things to the living God, who made heaven and earth and sea and everything in them.

verses.esv: In past generations he allowed all the nations to walk in their own ways.
verses.kjv: Who in times past suffered all nations to walk in their own ways.
verses.bsb: In past generations, He let all nations go their own way.

verses.esv: Yet he did not leave himself without witness, for he did good by giving you rains from heaven and fruitful seasons, satisfying your hearts with food and gladness.”
verses.kjv: Nevertheless he left not himself without witness, in that he did good, and gave us rain from heaven, and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness.
verses.bsb: Yet He has not left Himself without testimony to His goodness: He gives you rain from heaven and fruitful seasons, filling your hearts with food and gladness.”

verses.esv: Even with these words they scarcely restrained the people from offering sacrifice to them.
verses.kjv: And with these sayings scarce restrained they the people, that they had not done sacrifice unto them.
verses.bsb: Even with these words, Paul and Barnabas could hardly stop the crowds from sacrificing to them.

verses.esv: But Jews came from Antioch and Iconium, and having persuaded the crowds, they stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing that he was dead.
verses.kjv: ¶ And there came thither certain Jews from Antioch and Iconium, who persuaded the people, and, having stoned Paul, drew him out of the city, supposing he had been dead.
verses.bsb: Then some Jews arrived from Antioch and Iconium and won over the crowds. They stoned Paul and dragged him outside the city, presuming he was dead.

verses.esv: But when the disciples gathered about him, he rose up and entered the city, and on the next day he went on with Barnabas to Derbe.
verses.kjv: Howbeit, as the disciples stood round about him, he rose up, and came into the city: and the next day he departed with Barnabas to Derbe.
verses.bsb: But after the disciples had gathered around him, he got up and went back into the city. And the next day he left with Barnabas for Derbe.

verses.esv: When they had preached the gospel to that city and had made many disciples, they returned to Lystra and to Iconium and to Antioch,
verses.kjv: And when they had preached the gospel to that city, and had taught many, they returned again to Lystra, and to Iconium, and Antioch,
verses.bsb: They preached the gospel to that city and made many disciples. Then they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch,

verses.esv: strengthening the souls of the disciples, encouraging them to continue in the faith, and saying that through many tribulations we must enter the kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: Confirming the souls of the disciples, and exhorting them to continue in the faith, and that we must through much tribulation enter into the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: strengthening the souls of the disciples and encouraging them to continue in the faith. “We must endure many hardships to enter the kingdom of God,” they said.

verses.esv: And when they had appointed elders for them in every church, with prayer and fasting they committed them to the Lord in whom they had believed.
verses.kjv: And when they had ordained them elders in every church, and had prayed with fasting, they commended them to the Lord, on whom they believed.
verses.bsb: Paul and Barnabas appointed elders for them in each church, praying and fasting as they entrusted them to the Lord, in whom they had believed.

verses.esv: Then they passed through Pisidia and came to Pamphylia.
verses.kjv: And after they had passed throughout Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
verses.bsb: After passing through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.

verses.esv: And when they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia,
verses.kjv: And when they had preached the word in Perga, they went down into Attalia:
verses.bsb: And when they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.

verses.esv: and from there they sailed to Antioch, where they had been commended to the grace of God for the work that they had fulfilled.
verses.kjv: And thence sailed to Antioch, from whence they had been recommended to the grace of God for the work which they fulfilled.
verses.bsb: From Attalia they sailed to Antioch, where they had been commended to the grace of God for the work they had just completed.

verses.esv: And when they arrived and gathered the church together, they declared all that God had done with them, and how he had opened a door of faith to the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: And when they were come, and had gathered the church together, they rehearsed all that God had done with them, and how he had opened the door of faith unto the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: When they arrived, they gathered the church together and reported all that God had done through them, and how He had opened the door of faith to the Gentiles.

verses.esv: And they remained no little time with the disciples.
verses.kjv: And there they abode long time with the disciples.
verses.bsb: And they spent a long time there with the disciples.

verses.esv: But some men came down from Judea and were teaching the brothers, “Unless you are circumcised according to the custom of Moses, you cannot be saved.”
verses.kjv: AND certain men which came down from Judæa taught the brethren, and said, Except ye be circumcised after the manner of Moses, ye cannot be saved.
verses.bsb: Then some men came down from Judea and were teaching the brothers, “Unless you are circumcised according to the custom of Moses, you cannot be saved.”

verses.esv: And after Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and debate with them, Paul and Barnabas and some of the others were appointed to go up to Jerusalem to the apostles and the elders about this question.
verses.kjv: When therefore Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and disputation with them, they determined that Paul and Barnabas, and certain other of them, should go up to Jerusalem unto the apostles and elders about this question.
verses.bsb: And after engaging these men in sharp debate, Paul and Barnabas were appointed, along with some other believers, to go up to Jerusalem to see the apostles and elders about this question.

verses.esv: So, being sent on their way by the church, they passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria, describing in detail the conversion of the Gentiles, and brought great joy to all the brothers.
verses.kjv: And being brought on their way by the church, they passed through Phenice and Samaria, declaring the conversion of the Gentiles: and they caused great joy unto all the brethren.
verses.bsb: Sent on their way by the church, they passed through Phoenicia and Samaria, recounting the conversion of the Gentiles and bringing great joy to all the brothers.

verses.esv: When they came to Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church and the apostles and the elders, and they declared all that God had done with them.
verses.kjv: And when they were come to Jerusalem, they were received of the church, and of the apostles and elders, and they declared all things that God had done with them.
verses.bsb: On their arrival in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church and apostles and elders, to whom they reported all that God had done through them.

verses.esv: But some believers who belonged to the party of the Pharisees rose up and said, “It is necessary to circumcise them and to order them to keep the law of Moses.”
verses.kjv: But there rose up certain of the sect of the Pharisees which believed, saying, That it was needful to circumcise them, and to command them to keep the law of Moses.
verses.bsb: But some believers from the party of the Pharisees stood up and declared, “The Gentiles must be circumcised and required to obey the law of Moses.”

verses.esv: The apostles and the elders were gathered together to consider this matter.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the apostles and elders came together for to consider of this matter.
verses.bsb: So the apostles and elders met to look into this matter.

verses.esv: And after there had been much debate, Peter stood up and said to them, “Brothers, you know that in the early days God made a choice among you, that by my mouth the Gentiles should hear the word of the gospel and believe.
verses.kjv: And when there had been much disputing, Peter rose up, and said unto them, Men and brethren, ye know how that a good while ago God made choice among us, that the Gentiles by my mouth should hear the word of the gospel, and believe.
verses.bsb: After much discussion, Peter got up and said to them, “Brothers, you know that in the early days God made a choice among you that the Gentiles would hear from my lips the message of the gospel and believe.

verses.esv: And God, who knows the heart, bore witness to them, by giving them the Holy Spirit just as he did to us,
verses.kjv: And God, which knoweth the hearts, bare them witness, giving them the Holy Ghost, even as he did unto us;
verses.bsb: And God, who knows the heart, showed His approval by giving the Holy Spirit to them, just as He did to us.

verses.esv: and he made no distinction between us and them, having cleansed their hearts by faith.
verses.kjv: And put no difference between us and them, purifying their hearts by faith.
verses.bsb: He made no distinction between us and them, for He cleansed their hearts by faith.

verses.esv: Now, therefore, why are you putting God to the test by placing a yoke on the neck of the disciples that neither our fathers nor we have been able to bear?
verses.kjv: Now therefore why tempt ye God, to put a yoke upon the neck of the disciples, which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?
verses.bsb: Now then, why do you test God by placing on the necks of the disciples a yoke that neither we nor our fathers have been able to bear?

verses.esv: But we believe that we will be saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus, just as they will.”
verses.kjv: But we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ we shall be saved, even as they.
verses.bsb: On the contrary, we believe it is through the grace of the Lord Jesus that we are saved, just as they are.”

verses.esv: And all the assembly fell silent, and they listened to Barnabas and Paul as they related what signs and wonders God had done through them among the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then all the multitude kept silence, and gave audience to Barnabas and Paul, declaring what miracles and wonders God had wrought among the Gentiles by them.
verses.bsb: The whole assembly fell silent as they listened to Barnabas and Paul describing the signs and wonders God had done among the Gentiles through them.

verses.esv: After they finished speaking, James replied, “Brothers, listen to me.
verses.kjv: ¶ And after they had held their peace, James answered, saying, Men and brethren, hearken unto me:
verses.bsb: When they had finished speaking, James declared, “Brothers, listen to me!

verses.esv: Simeon has related how God first visited the Gentiles, to take from them a people for his name.
verses.kjv: Simeon hath declared how God at the first did visit the Gentiles, to take out of them a people for his name.
verses.bsb: Simon has told us how God first visited the Gentiles to take from them a people to be His own.

verses.esv: And with this the words of the prophets agree, just as it is written,
verses.kjv: And to this agree the words of the prophets; as it is written,
verses.bsb: The words of the prophets agree with this, as it is written:

verses.esv: “‘After this I will return, and I will rebuild the tent of David that has fallen; I will rebuild its ruins, and I will restore it,
verses.kjv: After this I will return, and will build again the tabernacle of David, which is fallen down; and I will build again the ruins thereof, and I will set it up:
verses.bsb: ‘After this I will return and rebuild the fallen tent of David. Its ruins I will rebuild, and I will restore it,

verses.esv: that the remnant of mankind may seek the Lord, and all the Gentiles who are called by my name, says the Lord, who makes these things
verses.kjv: That the residue of men might seek after the Lord, and all the Gentiles, upon whom my name is called, saith the Lord, who doeth all these things.
verses.bsb: so that the remnant of men may seek the Lord, and all the Gentiles who are called by My name, says the Lord who does these things

verses.esv: known from of old.’
verses.kjv: Known unto God are all his works from the beginning of the world.
verses.bsb: that have been known for ages.’

verses.esv: Therefore my judgment is that we should not trouble those of the Gentiles who turn to God,
verses.kjv: Wherefore my sentence is, that we trouble not them, which from among the Gentiles are turned to God:
verses.bsb: It is my judgment, therefore, that we should not cause trouble for the Gentiles who are turning to God.

verses.esv: but should write to them to abstain from the things polluted by idols, and from sexual immorality, and from what has been strangled, and from blood.
verses.kjv: But that we write unto them, that they abstain from pollutions of idols, and from fornication, and from things strangled, and from blood.
verses.bsb: Instead, we should write and tell them to abstain from food polluted by idols, from sexual immorality, from the meat of strangled animals, and from blood.

verses.esv: For from ancient generations Moses has had in every city those who proclaim him, for he is read every Sabbath in the synagogues.”
verses.kjv: For Moses of old time hath in every city them that preach him, being read in the synagogues every sabbath day.
verses.bsb: For Moses has been proclaimed in every city from ancient times and is read in the synagogues on every Sabbath.”

verses.esv: Then it seemed good to the apostles and the elders, with the whole church, to choose men from among them and send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas. They sent Judas called Barsabbas, and Silas, leading men among the brothers,
verses.kjv: Then pleased it the apostles and elders, with the whole church, to send chosen men of their own company to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas; namely, Judas surnamed Barsabas, and Silas, chief men among the brethren:
verses.bsb: Then the apostles and elders, with the whole church, decided to select men from among them to send to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Judas called Barsabbas and Silas, two leaders among the brothers,

verses.esv: with the following letter: “The brothers, both the apostles and the elders, to the brothers who are of the Gentiles in Antioch and Syria and Cilicia, greetings.
verses.kjv: And they wrote letters by them after this manner; The apostles and elders and brethren send greeting unto the brethren which are of the Gentiles in Antioch and Syria and Cilicia:
verses.bsb: and sent them with this letter:  The apostles and the elders, your brothers,  To the brothers among the Gentiles in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia:  Greetings.

verses.esv: Since we have heard that some persons have gone out from us and troubled you with words, unsettling your minds, although we gave them no instructions,
verses.kjv: Forasmuch as we have heard, that certain which went out from us have troubled you with words, subverting your souls, saying, Ye must be circumcised, and keep the law: to whom we gave no such commandment:
verses.bsb: It has come to our attention that some went out from us without our authorization and unsettled you, troubling your minds by what they said.

verses.esv: it has seemed good to us, having come to one accord, to choose men and send them to you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,
verses.kjv: It seemed good unto us, being assembled with one accord, to send chosen men unto you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,
verses.bsb: So we all agreed to choose men to send to you along with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,

verses.esv: men who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: Men that have hazarded their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: men who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.esv: We have therefore sent Judas and Silas, who themselves will tell you the same things by word of mouth.
verses.kjv: We have sent therefore Judas and Silas, who shall also tell you the same things by mouth.
verses.bsb: Therefore we are sending Judas and Silas to tell you in person the same things we are writing.

verses.esv: For it has seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us to lay on you no greater burden than these requirements:
verses.kjv: For it seemed good to the Holy Ghost, and to us, to lay upon you no greater burden than these necessary things;
verses.bsb: It seemed good to the Holy Spirit and to us not to burden you with anything beyond these essential requirements:

verses.esv: that you abstain from what has been sacrificed to idols, and from blood, and from what has been strangled, and from sexual immorality. If you keep yourselves from these, you will do well. Farewell.”
verses.kjv: That ye abstain from meats offered to idols, and from blood, and from things strangled, and from fornication: from which if ye keep yourselves, ye shall do well. Fare ye well.
verses.bsb: You must abstain from food sacrificed to idols, from blood, from the meat of strangled animals, and from sexual immorality. You will do well to avoid these things.  Farewell.

verses.esv: So when they were sent off, they went down to Antioch, and having gathered the congregation together, they delivered the letter.
verses.kjv: So when they were dismissed, they came to Antioch: and when they had gathered the multitude together, they delivered the epistle:
verses.bsb: So the men were sent off and went down to Antioch, where they assembled the congregation and delivered the letter.

verses.esv: And when they had read it, they rejoiced because of its encouragement.
verses.kjv: Which when they had read, they rejoiced for the consolation.
verses.bsb: When the people read it, they rejoiced at its encouraging message.

verses.esv: And Judas and Silas, who were themselves prophets, encouraged and strengthened the brothers with many words.
verses.kjv: And Judas and Silas, being prophets also themselves, exhorted the brethren with many words, and confirmed them.
verses.bsb: Judas and Silas, who themselves were prophets, said much to encourage and strengthen the brothers.

verses.esv: And after they had spent some time, they were sent off in peace by the brothers to those who had sent them.
verses.kjv: And after they had tarried there a space, they were let go in peace from the brethren unto the apostles.
verses.bsb: After spending some time there, they were sent off by the brothers in peace to return to those who had sent them.

verses.esv: But Paul and Barnabas remained in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.
verses.kjv: Paul also and Barnabas continued in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.
verses.bsb: But Paul and Barnabas remained at Antioch, along with many others, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord.

verses.esv: And after some days Paul said to Barnabas, “Let us return and visit the brothers in every city where we proclaimed the word of the Lord, and see how they are.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And some days after Paul said unto Barnabas, Let us go again and visit our brethren in every city where we have preached the word of the Lord, and see how they do.
verses.bsb: Some time later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let us go back and visit the brothers in every town where we proclaimed the word of the Lord, to see how they are doing.”

verses.esv: Now Barnabas wanted to take with them John called Mark.
verses.kjv: And Barnabas determined to take with them John, whose surname was Mark.
verses.bsb: Barnabas wanted to take John, also called Mark.

verses.esv: But Paul thought best not to take with them one who had withdrawn from them in Pamphylia and had not gone with them to the work.
verses.kjv: But Paul thought not good to take him with them, who departed from them from Pamphylia, and went not with them to the work.
verses.bsb: But Paul thought it best not to take him, because he had deserted them in Pamphylia and had not accompanied them in the work.

verses.esv: And there arose a sharp disagreement, so that they separated from each other. Barnabas took Mark with him and sailed away to Cyprus,
verses.kjv: And the contention was so sharp between them, that they departed asunder one from the other: and so Barnabas took Mark, and sailed unto Cyprus;
verses.bsb: Their disagreement was so sharp that they parted company. Barnabas took Mark and sailed for Cyprus,

verses.esv: but Paul chose Silas and departed, having been commended by the brothers to the grace of the Lord.
verses.kjv: And Paul chose Silas, and departed, being recommended by the brethren unto the grace of God.
verses.bsb: but Paul chose Silas and left, commended by the brothers to the grace of the Lord.

verses.esv: And he went through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
verses.kjv: And he went through Syria and Cilicia, confirming the churches.
verses.bsb: And he traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.

verses.esv: Paul came also to Derbe and to Lystra. A disciple was there, named Timothy, the son of a Jewish woman who was a believer, but his father was a Greek.
verses.kjv: THEN came he to Derbe and Lystra: and, behold, a certain disciple was there, named Timotheus, the son of a certain woman, which was a Jewess, and believed; but his father was a Greek:
verses.bsb: Paul came to Derbe and then to Lystra, where he found a disciple named Timothy, the son of a believing Jewish woman and a Greek father.

verses.esv: He was well spoken of by the brothers at Lystra and Iconium.
verses.kjv: Which was well reported of by the brethren that were at Lystra and Iconium.
verses.bsb: The brothers in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.

verses.esv: Paul wanted Timothy to accompany him, and he took him and circumcised him because of the Jews who were in those places, for they all knew that his father was a Greek.
verses.kjv: Him would Paul have to go forth with him; and took and circumcised him because of the Jews which were in those quarters: for they knew all that his father was a Greek.
verses.bsb: Paul wanted Timothy to accompany him, so he took him and circumcised him on account of the Jews in that area, for they all knew that his father was a Greek.

verses.esv: As they went on their way through the cities, they delivered to them for observance the decisions that had been reached by the apostles and elders who were in Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: And as they went through the cities, they delivered them the decrees for to keep, that were ordained of the apostles and elders which were at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: As they went from town to town, they delivered the decisions handed down by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem for the people to obey.

verses.esv: So the churches were strengthened in the faith, and they increased in numbers daily.
verses.kjv: And so were the churches established in the faith, and increased in number daily.
verses.bsb: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and grew daily in numbers.

verses.esv: And they went through the region of Phrygia and Galatia, having been forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia.
verses.kjv: Now when they had gone throughout Phrygia and the region of Galatia, and were forbidden of the Holy Ghost to preach the word in Asia,
verses.bsb: After the Holy Spirit had prevented them from speaking the word in the province of Asia, they traveled through the region of Phrygia and Galatia.

verses.esv: And when they had come up to Mysia, they attempted to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus did not allow them.
verses.kjv: After they were come to Mysia, they assayed to go into Bithynia: but the Spirit suffered them not.
verses.bsb: And when they came to the border of Mysia, they tried to enter Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not permit them.

verses.esv: So, passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas.
verses.kjv: And they passing by Mysia came down to Troas.
verses.bsb: So they passed by Mysia and went down to Troas.

verses.esv: And a vision appeared to Paul in the night: a man of Macedonia was standing there, urging him and saying, “Come over to Macedonia and help us.”
verses.kjv: And a vision appeared to Paul in the night; There stood a man of Macedonia, and prayed him, saying, Come over into Macedonia, and help us.
verses.bsb: During the night, Paul had a vision of a man of Macedonia standing and pleading with him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us.”

verses.esv: And when Paul had seen the vision, immediately we sought to go on into Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to preach the gospel to them.
verses.kjv: And after he had seen the vision, immediately we endeavoured to go into Macedonia, assuredly gathering that the Lord had called us for to preach the gospel unto them.
verses.bsb: As soon as Paul had seen the vision, we got ready to leave for Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to preach the gospel to them.

verses.esv: So, setting sail from Troas, we made a direct voyage to Samothrace, and the following day to Neapolis,
verses.kjv: Therefore loosing from Troas, we came with a straight course to Samothracia, and the next day to Neapolis;
verses.bsb: We sailed from Troas straight to Samothrace, and the following day on to Neapolis.

verses.esv: and from there to Philippi, which is a leading city of the district of Macedonia and a Roman colony. We remained in this city some days.
verses.kjv: And from thence to Philippi, which is the chief city of that part of Macedonia, and a colony: and we were in that city abiding certain days.
verses.bsb: From there we went to the Roman colony of Philippi, the leading city of that district of Macedonia. And we stayed there several days.

verses.esv: And on the Sabbath day we went outside the gate to the riverside, where we supposed there was a place of prayer, and we sat down and spoke to the women who had come together.
verses.kjv: And on the sabbath we went out of the city by a river side, where prayer was wont to be made; and we sat down, and spake unto the women which resorted thither.
verses.bsb: On the Sabbath we went outside the city gate along the river, where it was customary to find a place of prayer. After sitting down, we spoke to the women who had gathered there.

verses.esv: One who heard us was a woman named Lydia, from the city of Thyatira, a seller of purple goods, who was a worshiper of God. The Lord opened her heart to pay attention to what was said by Paul.
verses.kjv: ¶ And a certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, which worshipped God, heard us: whose heart the Lord opened, that she attended unto the things which were spoken of Paul.
verses.bsb: Among those listening was a woman named Lydia, a dealer in purple cloth from the city of Thyatira, who was a worshiper of God. The Lord opened her heart to respond to Paul’s message.

verses.esv: And after she was baptized, and her household as well, she urged us, saying, “If you have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come to my house and stay.” And she prevailed upon us.
verses.kjv: And when she was baptized, and her household, she besought us, saying, If ye have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come into my house, and abide there. And she constrained us.
verses.bsb: And when she and her household had been baptized, she urged us, “If you consider me a believer in the Lord, come and stay at my house.” And she persuaded us.

verses.esv: As we were going to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl who had a spirit of divination and brought her owners much gain by fortune-telling.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, as we went to prayer, a certain damsel possessed with a spirit of divination met us, which brought her masters much gain by soothsaying:
verses.bsb: One day as we were going to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl with a spirit of divination, who earned a large income for her masters by fortune-telling.

verses.esv: She followed Paul and us, crying out, “These men are servants of the Most High God, who proclaim to you the way of salvation.”
verses.kjv: The same followed Paul and us, and cried, saying, These men are the servants of the most high God, which shew unto us the way of salvation.
verses.bsb: This girl followed Paul and the rest of us, shouting, “These men are servants of the Most High God, who are proclaiming to you the way of salvation!”

verses.esv: And this she kept doing for many days. Paul, having become greatly annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, “I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her.” And it came out that very hour.
verses.kjv: And this did she many days. But Paul, being grieved, turned and said to the spirit, I command thee in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her. And he came out the same hour.
verses.bsb: She continued this for many days. Eventually Paul grew so aggravated that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ I command you to come out of her!” And the spirit left her at that very moment.

verses.esv: But when her owners saw that their hope of gain was gone, they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them into the marketplace before the rulers.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when her masters saw that the hope of their gains was gone, they caught Paul and Silas, and drew them into the marketplace unto the rulers,
verses.bsb: When the girl’s owners saw that their hope of making money was gone, they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them before the authorities in the marketplace.

verses.esv: And when they had brought them to the magistrates, they said, “These men are Jews, and they are disturbing our city.
verses.kjv: And brought them to the magistrates, saying, These men, being Jews, do exceedingly trouble our city,
verses.bsb: They brought them to the magistrates and said, “These men are Jews and are throwing our city into turmoil

verses.esv: They advocate customs that are not lawful for us as Romans to accept or practice.”
verses.kjv: And teach customs, which are not lawful for us to receive, neither to observe, being Romans.
verses.bsb: by promoting customs that are unlawful for us Romans to adopt or practice.”

verses.esv: The crowd joined in attacking them, and the magistrates tore the garments off them and gave orders to beat them with rods.
verses.kjv: And the multitude rose up together against them: and the magistrates rent off their clothes, and commanded to beat them.
verses.bsb: The crowd joined in the attack against Paul and Silas, and the magistrates ordered that they be stripped and beaten with rods.

verses.esv: And when they had inflicted many blows upon them, they threw them into prison, ordering the jailer to keep them safely.
verses.kjv: And when they had laid many stripes upon them, they cast them into prison, charging the jailor to keep them safely:
verses.bsb: And after striking them with many blows, they threw them into prison and ordered the jailer to guard them securely.

verses.esv: Having received this order, he put them into the inner prison and fastened their feet in the stocks.
verses.kjv: Who, having received such a charge, thrust them into the inner prison, and made their feet fast in the stocks.
verses.bsb: On receiving this order, he placed them in the inner cell and fastened their feet in the stocks.

verses.esv: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the prisoners were listening to them,
verses.kjv: ¶ And at midnight Paul and Silas prayed, and sang praises unto God: and the prisoners heard them.
verses.bsb: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the other prisoners were listening to them.

verses.esv: and suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken. And immediately all the doors were opened, and everyone's bonds were unfastened.
verses.kjv: And suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken: and immediately all the doors were opened, and every one’s bands were loosed.
verses.bsb: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the foundations of the prison. At once all the doors flew open and everyone’s chains came loose.

verses.esv: When the jailer woke and saw that the prison doors were open, he drew his sword and was about to kill himself, supposing that the prisoners had escaped.
verses.kjv: And the keeper of the prison awaking out of his sleep, and seeing the prison doors open, he drew out his sword, and would have killed himself, supposing that the prisoners had been fled.
verses.bsb: When the jailer woke up and saw the prison doors open, he drew his sword and was about to kill himself, presuming that the prisoners had escaped.

verses.esv: But Paul cried with a loud voice, “Do not harm yourself, for we are all here.”
verses.kjv: But Paul cried with a loud voice, saying, Do thyself no harm: for we are all here.
verses.bsb: But Paul called out in a loud voice, “Do not harm yourself! We are all here!”

verses.esv: And the jailer called for lights and rushed in, and trembling with fear he fell down before Paul and Silas.
verses.kjv: Then he called for a light, and sprang in, and came trembling, and fell down before Paul and Silas,
verses.bsb: Calling for lights, the jailer rushed in and fell trembling before Paul and Silas.

verses.esv: Then he brought them out and said, “Sirs, what must I do to be saved?”
verses.kjv: And brought them out, and said, Sirs, what must I do to be saved?
verses.bsb: Then he brought them out and asked, “Sirs, what must I do to be saved?”

verses.esv: And they said, “Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved, you and your household.”
verses.kjv: And they said, Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved, and thy house.
verses.bsb: They replied, “Believe in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved, you and your household.”

verses.esv: And they spoke the word of the Lord to him and to all who were in his house.
verses.kjv: And they spake unto him the word of the Lord, and to all that were in his house.
verses.bsb: Then Paul and Silas spoke the word of the Lord to him and to everyone in his house.

verses.esv: And he took them the same hour of the night and washed their wounds; and he was baptized at once, he and all his family.
verses.kjv: And he took them the same hour of the night, and washed their stripes; and was baptized, he and all his, straightway.
verses.bsb: At that hour of the night, the jailer took them and washed their wounds. And without delay, he and all his household were baptized.

verses.esv: Then he brought them up into his house and set food before them. And he rejoiced along with his entire household that he had believed in God.
verses.kjv: And when he had brought them into his house, he set meat before them, and rejoiced, believing in God with all his house.
verses.bsb: Then he brought them into his home and set a meal before them. So he and all his household rejoiced that they had come to believe in God.

verses.esv: But when it was day, the magistrates sent the police, saying, “Let those men go.”
verses.kjv: And when it was day, the magistrates sent the serjeants, saying, Let those men go.
verses.bsb: When daylight came, the magistrates sent their officers with the order: “Release those men.”

verses.esv: And the jailer reported these words to Paul, saying, “The magistrates have sent to let you go. Therefore come out now and go in peace.”
verses.kjv: And the keeper of the prison told this saying to Paul, The magistrates have sent to let you go: now therefore depart, and go in peace.
verses.bsb: The jailer informed Paul: “The magistrates have sent orders to release you. Now you may go on your way in peace.”

verses.esv: But Paul said to them, “They have beaten us publicly, uncondemned, men who are Roman citizens, and have thrown us into prison; and do they now throw us out secretly? No! Let them come themselves and take us out.”
verses.kjv: But Paul said unto them, They have beaten us openly uncondemned, being Romans, and have cast us into prison; and now do they thrust us out privily? nay verily; but let them come themselves and fetch us out.
verses.bsb: But Paul said to the officers, “They beat us publicly without a trial and threw us into prison, even though we are Roman citizens. And now do they want to send us away secretly? Absolutely not! Let them come themselves and escort us out!”

verses.esv: The police reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Roman citizens.
verses.kjv: And the serjeants told these words unto the magistrates: and they feared, when they heard that they were Romans.
verses.bsb: So the officers relayed this message to the magistrates, who were alarmed to hear that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens.

verses.esv: So they came and apologized to them. And they took them out and asked them to leave the city.
verses.kjv: And they came and besought them, and brought them out, and desired them to depart out of the city.
verses.bsb: They came to appease them and led them out, requesting that they leave the city.

verses.esv: So they went out of the prison and visited Lydia. And when they had seen the brothers, they encouraged them and departed.
