verses.bsb: I fell to the ground and heard a voice say to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why do you persecute Me?’

verses.esv: And I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ And he said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting.’
verses.kjv: And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me, I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.
verses.bsb: ‘Who are You, Lord?’ I asked.  ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting,’ He replied.

verses.esv: Now those who were with me saw the light but did not understand the voice of the one who was speaking to me.
verses.kjv: And they that were with me saw indeed the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me.
verses.bsb: My companions saw the light, but they could not understand the voice of the One speaking to me.

verses.esv: And I said, ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ And the Lord said to me, ‘Rise, and go into Damascus, and there you will be told all that is appointed for you to do.’
verses.kjv: And I said, What shall I do, Lord? And the Lord said unto me, Arise, and go into Damascus; and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.
verses.bsb: Then I asked, ‘What should I do, Lord?’  ‘Get up and go into Damascus,’ He told me. ‘There you will be told all that you have been appointed to do.’

verses.esv: And since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, I was led by the hand by those who were with me, and came into Damascus.
verses.kjv: And when I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them that were with me, I came into Damascus.
verses.bsb: Because the brilliance of the light had blinded me, my companions led me by the hand into Damascus.

verses.esv: “And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, well spoken of by all the Jews who lived there,
verses.kjv: And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, having a good report of all the Jews which dwelt there,
verses.bsb: There a man named Ananias, a devout observer of the law who was highly regarded by all the Jews living there,

verses.esv: came to me, and standing by me said to me, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight.’ And at that very hour I received my sight and saw him.
verses.kjv: Came unto me, and stood, and said unto me, Brother Saul, receive thy sight. And the same hour I looked up upon him.
verses.bsb: came and stood beside me. ‘Brother Saul,’ he said, ‘receive your sight.’ And at that moment I could see him.

verses.esv: And he said, ‘The God of our fathers appointed you to know his will, to see the Righteous One and to hear a voice from his mouth;
verses.kjv: And he said, The God of our fathers hath chosen thee, that thou shouldest know his will, and see that Just One, and shouldest hear the voice of his mouth.
verses.bsb: Then he said, ‘The God of our fathers has appointed you to know His will, and to see the Righteous One, and to hear His voice.

verses.esv: for you will be a witness for him to everyone of what you have seen and heard.
verses.kjv: For thou shalt be his witness unto all men of what thou hast seen and heard.
verses.bsb: You will be His witness to everyone of what you have seen and heard.

verses.esv: And now why do you wait? Rise and be baptized and wash away your sins, calling on his name.’
verses.kjv: And now why tarriest thou? arise, and be baptized, and wash away thy sins, calling on the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: And now what are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and wash your sins away, calling on His name.’

verses.esv: “When I had returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, when I was come again to Jerusalem, even while I prayed in the temple, I was in a trance;
verses.bsb: Later, when I had returned to Jerusalem and was praying at the temple, I fell into a trance

verses.esv: and saw him saying to me, ‘Make haste and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
verses.kjv: And saw him saying unto me, Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem: for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me.
verses.bsb: and saw the Lord saying to me, ‘Hurry! Leave Jerusalem quickly, because the people here will not accept your testimony about Me.’

verses.esv: And I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that in one synagogue after another I imprisoned and beat those who believed in you.
verses.kjv: And I said, Lord, they know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue them that believed on thee:
verses.bsb: ‘Lord,’ I answered, ‘they know very well that in one synagogue after another I imprisoned and beat those who believed in You.

verses.esv: And when the blood of Stephen your witness was being shed, I myself was standing by and approving and watching over the garments of those who killed him.’
verses.kjv: And when the blood of thy martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting unto his death, and kept the raiment of them that slew him.
verses.bsb: And when the blood of Your witness Stephen was shed, I stood there giving my approval and watching over the garments of those who killed him.’

verses.esv: And he said to me, ‘Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’”
verses.kjv: And he said unto me, Depart: for I will send thee far hence unto the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: Then He said to me, ‘Go! I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’”

verses.esv: Up to this word they listened to him. Then they raised their voices and said, “Away with such a fellow from the earth! For he should not be allowed to live.”
verses.kjv: And they gave him audience unto this word, and then lifted up their voices, and said, Away with such a fellow from the earth: for it is not fit that he should live.
verses.bsb: The crowd listened to Paul until he made this statement. Then they lifted up their voices and shouted, “Rid the earth of him! He is not fit to live!”

verses.esv: And as they were shouting and throwing off their cloaks and flinging dust into the air,
verses.kjv: And as they cried out, and cast off their clothes, and threw dust into the air,
verses.bsb: As they were shouting and throwing off their cloaks and tossing dust into the air,

verses.esv: the tribune ordered him to be brought into the barracks, saying that he should be examined by flogging, to find out why they were shouting against him like this.
verses.kjv: The chief captain commanded him to be brought into the castle, and bade that he should be examined by scourging; that he might know wherefore they cried so against him.
verses.bsb: the commander ordered that Paul be brought into the barracks. He directed that Paul be flogged and interrogated to determine the reason for this outcry against him.

verses.esv: But when they had stretched him out for the whips, Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, “Is it lawful for you to flog a man who is a Roman citizen and uncondemned?”
verses.kjv: And as they bound him with thongs, Paul said unto the centurion that stood by, Is it lawful for you to scourge a man that is a Roman, and uncondemned?
verses.bsb: But as they stretched him out to strap him down, Paul said to the centurion standing there, “Is it lawful for you to flog a Roman citizen without a trial?”

verses.esv: When the centurion heard this, he went to the tribune and said to him, “What are you about to do? For this man is a Roman citizen.”
verses.kjv: When the centurion heard that, he went and told the chief captain, saying, Take heed what thou doest: for this man is a Roman.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, the centurion went and reported it to the commander. “What are you going to do?” he said. “This man is a Roman citizen.”

verses.esv: So the tribune came and said to him, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” And he said, “Yes.”
verses.kjv: Then the chief captain came, and said unto him, Tell me, art thou a Roman? He said, Yea.
verses.bsb: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?”  “Yes,” he answered.

verses.esv: The tribune answered, “I bought this citizenship for a large sum.” Paul said, “But I am a citizen by birth.”
verses.kjv: And the chief captain answered, With a great sum obtained I this freedom. And Paul said, But I was free born.
verses.bsb: “I paid a high price for my citizenship,” said the commander.  “But I was born a citizen,” Paul replied.

verses.esv: So those who were about to examine him withdrew from him immediately, and the tribune also was afraid, for he realized that Paul was a Roman citizen and that he had bound him.
verses.kjv: Then straightway they departed from him which should have examined him: and the chief captain also was afraid, after he knew that he was a Roman, and because he had bound him.
verses.bsb: Then those who were about to interrogate Paul stepped back, and the commander himself was alarmed when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains.

verses.esv: But on the next day, desiring to know the real reason why he was being accused by the Jews, he unbound him and commanded the chief priests and all the council to meet, and he brought Paul down and set him before them.
verses.kjv: On the morrow, because he would have known the certainty wherefore he was accused of the Jews, he loosed him from his bands, and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear, and brought Paul down, and set him before them.
verses.bsb: The next day the commander, wanting to learn the real reason Paul was accused by the Jews, released him and ordered the chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin to assemble. Then he brought Paul down and had him stand before them.

verses.esv: And looking intently at the council, Paul said, “Brothers, I have lived my life before God in all good conscience up to this day.”
verses.kjv: AND Paul, earnestly beholding the council, said, Men and brethren, I have lived in all good conscience before God until this day.
verses.bsb: Paul looked directly at the Sanhedrin and said, “Brothers, I have conducted myself before God in all good conscience to this day.”

verses.esv: And the high priest Ananias commanded those who stood by him to strike him on the mouth.
verses.kjv: And the high priest Ananias commanded them that stood by him to smite him on the mouth.
verses.bsb: At this, the high priest Ananias ordered those standing near Paul to strike him on the mouth.

verses.esv: Then Paul said to him, “God is going to strike you, you whitewashed wall! Are you sitting to judge me according to the law, and yet contrary to the law you order me to be struck?”
verses.kjv: Then said Paul unto him, God shall smite thee, thou whited wall: for sittest thou to judge me after the law, and commandest me to be smitten contrary to the law?
verses.bsb: Then Paul said to him, “God will strike you, you whitewashed wall! You sit here to judge me according to the law, yet you yourself violate the law by commanding that I be struck.”

verses.esv: Those who stood by said, “Would you revile God's high priest?”
verses.kjv: And they that stood by said, Revilest thou God’s high priest?
verses.bsb: But those standing nearby said, “How dare you insult the high priest of God!”

verses.esv: And Paul said, “I did not know, brothers, that he was the high priest, for it is written, ‘You shall not speak evil of a ruler of your people.’”
verses.kjv: Then said Paul, I wist not, brethren, that he was the high priest: for it is written, Thou shalt not speak evil of the ruler of thy people.
verses.bsb: “Brothers,” Paul replied, “I was not aware that he was the high priest, for it is written: ‘Do not speak evil about the ruler of your people.’”

verses.esv: Now when Paul perceived that one part were Sadducees and the other Pharisees, he cried out in the council, “Brothers, I am a Pharisee, a son of Pharisees. It is with respect to the hope and the resurrection of the dead that I am on trial.”
verses.kjv: But when Paul perceived that the one part were Sadducees, and the other Pharisees, he cried out in the council, Men and brethren, I am a Pharisee, the son of a Pharisee: of the hope and resurrection of the dead I am called in question.
verses.bsb: Then Paul, knowing that some of them were Sadducees and others Pharisees, called out in the Sanhedrin, “Brothers, I am a Pharisee, the son of a Pharisee. It is because of my hope in the resurrection of the dead that I am on trial.”

verses.esv: And when he had said this, a dissension arose between the Pharisees and the Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.
verses.kjv: And when he had so said, there arose a dissension between the Pharisees and the Sadducees: and the multitude was divided.
verses.bsb: As soon as he had said this, a dispute broke out between the Pharisees and Sadducees, and the assembly was divided.

verses.esv: For the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, nor angel, nor spirit, but the Pharisees acknowledge them all.
verses.kjv: For the Sadducees say that there is no resurrection, neither angel, nor spirit: but the Pharisees confess both.
verses.bsb: For the Sadducees say that there is neither a resurrection, nor angels, nor spirits, but the Pharisees acknowledge them all.

verses.esv: Then a great clamor arose, and some of the scribes of the Pharisees' party stood up and contended sharply, “We find nothing wrong in this man. What if a spirit or an angel spoke to him?”
verses.kjv: And there arose a great cry: and the scribes that were of the Pharisees’ part arose, and strove, saying, We find no evil in this man: but if a spirit or an angel hath spoken to him, let us not fight against God.
verses.bsb: A great clamor arose, and some scribes from the party of the Pharisees got up and contended sharply, “We find nothing wrong with this man. What if a spirit or an angel has spoken to him?”

verses.esv: And when the dissension became violent, the tribune, afraid that Paul would be torn to pieces by them, commanded the soldiers to go down and take him away from among them by force and bring him into the barracks.
verses.kjv: And when there arose a great dissension, the chief captain, fearing lest Paul should have been pulled in pieces of them, commanded the soldiers to go down, and to take him by force from among them, and to bring him into the castle.
verses.bsb: The dispute grew so violent that the commander was afraid they would tear Paul to pieces. He ordered the soldiers to go down and remove him by force and bring him into the barracks.

verses.esv: The following night the Lord stood by him and said, “Take courage, for as you have testified to the facts about me in Jerusalem, so you must testify also in Rome.”
verses.kjv: And the night following the Lord stood by him, and said, Be of good cheer, Paul: for as thou hast testified of me in Jerusalem, so must thou bear witness also at Rome.
verses.bsb: The following night the Lord stood near Paul and said, “Take courage! As you have testified about Me in Jerusalem, so also you must testify in Rome.”

verses.esv: When it was day, the Jews made a plot and bound themselves by an oath neither to eat nor drink till they had killed Paul.
verses.kjv: And when it was day, certain of the Jews banded together, and bound themselves under a curse, saying that they would neither eat nor drink till they had killed Paul.
verses.bsb: When daylight came, the Jews formed a conspiracy and bound themselves with an oath not to eat or drink until they had killed Paul.

verses.esv: There were more than forty who made this conspiracy.
verses.kjv: And they were more than forty which had made this conspiracy.
verses.bsb: More than forty of them were involved in this plot.

verses.esv: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, “We have strictly bound ourselves by an oath to taste no food till we have killed Paul.
verses.kjv: And they came to the chief priests and elders, and said, We have bound ourselves under a great curse, that we will eat nothing until we have slain Paul.
verses.bsb: They went to the chief priests and elders and said, “We have bound ourselves with a solemn oath not to eat anything until we have killed Paul.

verses.esv: Now therefore you, along with the council, give notice to the tribune to bring him down to you, as though you were going to determine his case more exactly. And we are ready to kill him before he comes near.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore ye with the council signify to the chief captain that he bring him down unto you to morrow, as though ye would inquire something more perfectly concerning him: and we, or ever he come near, are ready to kill him.
verses.bsb: Now then, you and the Sanhedrin petition the commander to bring him down to you on the pretext of examining his case more carefully. We are ready to kill him on the way.”

verses.esv: Now the son of Paul's sister heard of their ambush, so he went and entered the barracks and told Paul.
verses.kjv: And when Paul’s sister’s son heard of their lying in wait, he went and entered into the castle, and told Paul.
verses.bsb: But when the son of Paul’s sister heard about the plot, he went into the barracks and told Paul.

verses.esv: Paul called one of the centurions and said, “Take this young man to the tribune, for he has something to tell him.”
verses.kjv: Then Paul called one of the centurions unto him, and said, Bring this young man unto the chief captain: for he hath a certain thing to tell him.
verses.bsb: Then Paul called one of the centurions and said, “Take this young man to the commander; he has something to tell him.”

verses.esv: So he took him and brought him to the tribune and said, “Paul the prisoner called me and asked me to bring this young man to you, as he has something to say to you.”
verses.kjv: So he took him, and brought him to the chief captain, and said, Paul the prisoner called me unto him, and prayed me to bring this young man unto thee, who hath something to say unto thee.
verses.bsb: So the centurion took him to the commander and said, “Paul the prisoner sent and asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to tell you.”

verses.esv: The tribune took him by the hand, and going aside asked him privately, “What is it that you have to tell me?”
verses.kjv: Then the chief captain took him by the hand, and went with him aside privately, and asked him, What is that thou hast to tell me?
verses.bsb: The commander took the young man by the hand, drew him aside, and asked, “What do you need to tell me?”

verses.esv: And he said, “The Jews have agreed to ask you to bring Paul down to the council tomorrow, as though they were going to inquire somewhat more closely about him.
verses.kjv: And he said, The Jews have agreed to desire thee that thou wouldest bring down Paul to morrow into the council, as though they would inquire somewhat of him more perfectly.
verses.bsb: He answered, “The Jews have agreed to ask you to bring Paul to the Sanhedrin tomorrow on the pretext of acquiring more information about him.

verses.esv: But do not be persuaded by them, for more than forty of their men are lying in ambush for him, who have bound themselves by an oath neither to eat nor drink till they have killed him. And now they are ready, waiting for your consent.”
verses.kjv: But do not thou yield unto them: for there lie in wait for him of them more than forty men, which have bound themselves with an oath, that they will neither eat nor drink till they have killed him: and now are they ready, looking for a promise from thee.
verses.bsb: Do not let them persuade you, because more than forty men are waiting to ambush him. They have bound themselves with an oath not to eat or drink until they have killed him; they are ready now, awaiting your consent.”

verses.esv: So the tribune dismissed the young man, charging him, “Tell no one that you have informed me of these things.”
verses.kjv: So the chief captain then let the young man depart, and charged him, See thou tell no man that thou hast shewed these things to me.
verses.bsb: So the commander dismissed the young man and instructed him, “Do not tell anyone that you have reported this to me.”

verses.esv: Then he called two of the centurions and said, “Get ready two hundred soldiers, with seventy horsemen and two hundred spearmen to go as far as Caesarea at the third hour of the night.
verses.kjv: And he called unto him two centurions, saying, Make ready two hundred soldiers to go to Cæsarea, and horsemen threescore and ten, and spearmen two hundred, at the third hour of the night;
verses.bsb: Then he called two of his centurions and said, “Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to go to Caesarea in the third hour of the night.

verses.esv: Also provide mounts for Paul to ride and bring him safely to Felix the governor.”
verses.kjv: And provide them beasts, that they may set Paul on, and bring him safe unto Felix the governor.
verses.bsb: Provide mounts for Paul to take him safely to Governor Felix.”

verses.esv: And he wrote a letter to this effect:
verses.kjv: And he wrote a letter after this manner:
verses.bsb: And he wrote the following letter:

verses.esv: “Claudius Lysias, to his Excellency the governor Felix, greetings.
verses.kjv: Claudius Lysias unto the most excellent governor Felix sendeth greeting.
verses.bsb: Claudius Lysias,  To His Excellency, Governor Felix:  Greetings.

verses.esv: This man was seized by the Jews and was about to be killed by them when I came upon them with the soldiers and rescued him, having learned that he was a Roman citizen.
verses.kjv: This man was taken of the Jews, and should have been killed of them: then came I with an army, and rescued him, having understood that he was a Roman.
verses.bsb: This man was seized by the Jews, and they were about to kill him when I came with my troops to rescue him. For I had learned that he is a Roman citizen,

verses.esv: And desiring to know the charge for which they were accusing him, I brought him down to their council.
verses.kjv: And when I would have known the cause wherefore they accused him, I brought him forth into their council:
verses.bsb: and since I wanted to understand their charges against him, I brought him down to their Sanhedrin.

verses.esv: I found that he was being accused about questions of their law, but charged with nothing deserving death or imprisonment.
verses.kjv: Whom I perceived to be accused of questions of their law, but to have nothing laid to his charge worthy of death or of bonds.
verses.bsb: I found that the accusation involved questions about their own law, but there was no charge worthy of death or imprisonment.

verses.esv: And when it was disclosed to me that there would be a plot against the man, I sent him to you at once, ordering his accusers also to state before you what they have against him.”
verses.kjv: And when it was told me how that the Jews laid wait for the man, I sent straightway to thee, and gave commandment to his accusers also to say before thee what they had against him. Farewell.
verses.bsb: When I was informed that there was a plot against the man, I sent him to you at once. I also instructed his accusers to present their case against him before you.

verses.esv: So the soldiers, according to their instructions, took Paul and brought him by night to Antipatris.
verses.kjv: Then the soldiers, as it was commanded them, took Paul, and brought him by night to Antipatris.
verses.bsb: So the soldiers followed their orders and brought Paul by night to Antipatris.

verses.esv: And on the next day they returned to the barracks, letting the horsemen go on with him.
verses.kjv: On the morrow they left the horsemen to go with him, and returned to the castle:
verses.bsb: The next day they returned to the barracks and let the horsemen go on with him.

verses.esv: When they had come to Caesarea and delivered the letter to the governor, they presented Paul also before him.
verses.kjv: Who, when they came to Cæsarea, and delivered the epistle to the governor, presented Paul also before him.
verses.bsb: When the horsemen arrived in Caesarea, they delivered the letter to the governor and presented Paul to him.

verses.esv: On reading the letter, he asked what province he was from. And when he learned that he was from Cilicia,
verses.kjv: And when the governor had read the letter, he asked of what province he was. And when he understood that he was of Cilicia;
verses.bsb: The governor read the letter and asked what province Paul was from. Learning that he was from Cilicia,

verses.esv: he said, “I will give you a hearing when your accusers arrive.” And he commanded him to be guarded in Herod's praetorium.
verses.kjv: I will hear thee, said he, when thine accusers are also come. And he commanded him to be kept in Herod’s judgment hall.
verses.bsb: he said, “I will hear your case when your accusers arrive.” Then he ordered that Paul be kept under guard in Herod’s Praetorium.

verses.esv: And after five days the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a spokesman, one Tertullus. They laid before the governor their case against Paul.
verses.kjv: AND after five days Ananias the high priest descended with the elders, and with a certain orator named Tertullus, who informed the governor against Paul.
verses.bsb: Five days later the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a lawyer named Tertullus, who presented to the governor their case against Paul.

verses.esv: And when he had been summoned, Tertullus began to accuse him, saying: “Since through you we enjoy much peace, and since by your foresight, most excellent Felix, reforms are being made for this nation,
verses.kjv: And when he was called forth, Tertullus began to accuse him, saying, Seeing that by thee we enjoy great quietness, and that very worthy deeds are done unto this nation by thy providence,
verses.bsb: When Paul had been called in, Tertullus opened the prosecution: “Because of you, we have enjoyed a lasting peace, and your foresight has brought improvements to this nation.

verses.esv: in every way and everywhere we accept this with all gratitude.
verses.kjv: We accept it always, and in all places, most noble Felix, with all thankfulness.
verses.bsb: In every way and everywhere, most excellent Felix, we acknowledge this with all gratitude.

verses.esv: But, to detain you no further, I beg you in your kindness to hear us briefly.
verses.kjv: Notwithstanding, that I be not further tedious unto thee, I pray thee that thou wouldest hear us of thy clemency a few words.
verses.bsb: But in order not to burden you any further, I beg your indulgence to hear us briefly.

verses.esv: For we have found this man a plague, one who stirs up riots among all the Jews throughout the world and is a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes.
verses.kjv: For we have found this man a pestilent fellow, and a mover of sedition among all the Jews throughout the world, and a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes:
verses.bsb: We have found this man to be a pestilence, stirring up dissension among the Jews all over the world. He is a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes,

verses.esv: He even tried to profane the temple, but we seized him.
verses.kjv: Who also hath gone about to profane the temple: whom we took, and would have judged according to our law.
verses.bsb: and he even tried to desecrate the temple; so we seized him.

verses.esv: By examining him yourself you will be able to find out from him about everything of which we accuse him.”
verses.kjv: Commanding his accusers to come unto thee: by examining of whom thyself mayest take knowledge of all these things, whereof we accuse him.
verses.bsb: By examining him yourself, you will be able to learn the truth about all our charges against him.”

verses.esv: The Jews also joined in the charge, affirming that all these things were so.
verses.kjv: And the Jews also assented, saying that these things were so.
verses.bsb: The Jews concurred, asserting that these charges were true.

verses.esv: And when the governor had nodded to him to speak, Paul replied: “Knowing that for many years you have been a judge over this nation, I cheerfully make my defense.
verses.kjv: Then Paul, after that the governor had beckoned unto him to speak, answered, Forasmuch as I know that thou hast been of many years a judge unto this nation, I do the more cheerfully answer for myself:
verses.bsb: When the governor motioned for Paul to speak, he began his response: “Knowing that you have been a judge over this nation for many years, I gladly make my defense.

verses.esv: You can verify that it is not more than twelve days since I went up to worship in Jerusalem,
verses.kjv: Because that thou mayest understand, that there are yet but twelve days since I went up to Jerusalem for to worship.
verses.bsb: You can verify for yourself that no more than twelve days ago I went up to Jerusalem to worship.

verses.esv: and they did not find me disputing with anyone or stirring up a crowd, either in the temple or in the synagogues or in the city.
verses.kjv: And they neither found me in the temple disputing with any man, neither raising up the people, neither in the synagogues, nor in the city:
verses.bsb: Yet my accusers did not find me debating with anyone in the temple or riling up a crowd in the synagogues or in the city.

verses.esv: Neither can they prove to you what they now bring up against me.
verses.kjv: Neither can they prove the things whereof they now accuse me.
verses.bsb: Nor can they prove to you any of their charges against me.

verses.esv: But this I confess to you, that according to the Way, which they call a sect, I worship the God of our fathers, believing everything laid down by the Law and written in the Prophets,
verses.kjv: But this I confess unto thee, that after the way which they call heresy, so worship I the God of my fathers, believing all things which are written in the law and in the prophets:
verses.bsb: I do confess to you, however, that I worship the God of our fathers according to the Way, which they call a sect. I believe everything that is laid down by the Law and written in the Prophets,

verses.esv: having a hope in God, which these men themselves accept, that there will be a resurrection of both the just and the unjust.
verses.kjv: And have hope toward God, which they themselves also allow, that there shall be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.
verses.bsb: and I have the same hope in God that they themselves cherish, that there will be a resurrection of both the righteous and the wicked.

verses.esv: So I always take pains to have a clear conscience toward both God and man.
verses.kjv: And herein do I exercise myself, to have always a conscience void of offence toward God, and toward men.
verses.bsb: In this hope, I strive always to maintain a clear conscience before God and man.

verses.esv: Now after several years I came to bring alms to my nation and to present offerings.
verses.kjv: Now after many years I came to bring alms to my nation, and offerings.
verses.bsb: After several years, then, I returned to Jerusalem to bring alms to my people and to present offerings.

verses.esv: While I was doing this, they found me purified in the temple, without any crowd or tumult. But some Jews from Asia—
verses.kjv: Whereupon certain Jews from Asia found me purified in the temple, neither with multitude, nor with tumult.
verses.bsb: At the time they found me in the temple, I was ceremonially clean and was not inciting a crowd or an uproar. But there are some Jews from the province of Asia

verses.esv: they ought to be here before you and to make an accusation, should they have anything against me.
verses.kjv: Who ought to have been here before thee, and object, if they had ought against me.
verses.bsb: who ought to appear before you and bring charges, if they have anything against me.

verses.esv: Or else let these men themselves say what wrongdoing they found when I stood before the council,
verses.kjv: Or else let these same here say, if they have found any evil doing in me, while I stood before the council,
verses.bsb: Otherwise, let these men state for themselves any crime they found in me when I stood before the Sanhedrin,

verses.esv: other than this one thing that I cried out while standing among them: ‘It is with respect to the resurrection of the dead that I am on trial before you this day.’”
verses.kjv: Except it be for this one voice, that I cried standing among them, Touching the resurrection of the dead I am called in question by you this day.
verses.bsb: unless it was this one thing I called out as I stood in their presence: ‘It is concerning the resurrection of the dead that I am on trial before you today.’”

verses.esv: But Felix, having a rather accurate knowledge of the Way, put them off, saying, “When Lysias the tribune comes down, I will decide your case.”
verses.kjv: And when Felix heard these things, having more perfect knowledge of that way, he deferred them, and said, When Lysias the chief captain shall come down, I will know the uttermost of your matter.
verses.bsb: Then Felix, who was well informed about the Way, adjourned the hearing and said, “When Lysias the commander comes, I will decide your case.”

verses.esv: Then he gave orders to the centurion that he should be kept in custody but have some liberty, and that none of his friends should be prevented from attending to his needs.
verses.kjv: And he commanded a centurion to keep Paul, and to let him have liberty, and that he should forbid none of his acquaintance to minister or come unto him.
verses.bsb: He ordered the centurion to keep Paul under guard, but to allow him some freedom and permit his friends to minister to his needs.

verses.esv: After some days Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, and he sent for Paul and heard him speak about faith in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: And after certain days, when Felix came with his wife Drusilla, which was a Jewess, he sent for Paul, and heard him concerning the faith in Christ.
verses.bsb: After several days, Felix returned with his wife Drusilla, who was a Jewess. He sent for Paul and listened to him speak about faith in Christ Jesus.

verses.esv: And as he reasoned about righteousness and self-control and the coming judgment, Felix was alarmed and said, “Go away for the present. When I get an opportunity I will summon you.”
verses.kjv: And as he reasoned of righteousness, temperance, and judgment to come, Felix trembled, and answered, Go thy way for this time; when I have a convenient season, I will call for thee.
verses.bsb: As Paul expounded on righteousness, self-control, and the coming judgment, Felix became frightened and said, “You may go for now. When I find the time, I will call for you.”

verses.esv: At the same time he hoped that money would be given him by Paul. So he sent for him often and conversed with him.
verses.kjv: He hoped also that money should have been given him of Paul, that he might loose him: wherefore he sent for him the oftener, and communed with him.
verses.bsb: At the same time, he was hoping that Paul would offer him a bribe. So he sent for Paul frequently and talked with him.

verses.esv: When two years had elapsed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. And desiring to do the Jews a favor, Felix left Paul in prison.
verses.kjv: But after two years Porcius Festus came into Felix’ room: and Felix, willing to shew the Jews a pleasure, left Paul bound.
verses.bsb: After two years had passed, Felix was succeeded by Porcius Festus. And wishing to do the Jews a favor, Felix left Paul in prison.

verses.esv: Now three days after Festus had arrived in the province, he went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
verses.kjv: NOW when Festus was come into the province, after three days he ascended from Cæsarea to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Three days after his arrival in the province, Festus went up from Caesarea to Jerusalem,

verses.esv: And the chief priests and the principal men of the Jews laid out their case against Paul, and they urged him,
verses.kjv: Then the high priest and the chief of the Jews informed him against Paul, and besought him,
verses.bsb: where the chief priests and Jewish leaders presented their case against Paul. They urged Festus

verses.esv: asking as a favor against Paul that he summon him to Jerusalem—because they were planning an ambush to kill him on the way.
verses.kjv: And desired favour against him, that he would send for him to Jerusalem, laying wait in the way to kill him.
verses.bsb: to grant them a concession against Paul by summoning him to Jerusalem, because they were preparing an ambush to kill him along the way.

verses.esv: Festus replied that Paul was being kept at Caesarea and that he himself intended to go there shortly.
verses.kjv: But Festus answered, that Paul should be kept at Cæsarea, and that he himself would depart shortly thither.
verses.bsb: But Festus replied, “Paul is being held in Caesarea, and I myself am going there soon.

verses.esv: “So,” said he, “let the men of authority among you go down with me, and if there is anything wrong about the man, let them bring charges against him.”
verses.kjv: Let them therefore, said he, which among you are able, go down with me, and accuse this man, if there be any wickedness in him.
verses.bsb: So if this man has done anything wrong, let some of your leaders come down with me and accuse him there.”

verses.esv: After he stayed among them not more than eight or ten days, he went down to Caesarea. And the next day he took his seat on the tribunal and ordered Paul to be brought.
verses.kjv: And when he had tarried among them more than ten days, he went down unto Cæsarea; and the next day sitting on the judgment seat commanded Paul to be brought.
verses.bsb: After spending no more than eight or ten days with them, Festus went down to Caesarea. The next day he sat on the judgment seat and ordered that Paul be brought in.

verses.esv: When he had arrived, the Jews who had come down from Jerusalem stood around him, bringing many and serious charges against him that they could not prove.
verses.kjv: And when he was come, the Jews which came down from Jerusalem stood round about, and laid many and grievous complaints against Paul, which they could not prove.
verses.bsb: When Paul arrived, the Jews who had come down from Jerusalem stood around him, bringing many serious charges that they could not prove.

verses.esv: Paul argued in his defense, “Neither against the law of the Jews, nor against the temple, nor against Caesar have I committed any offense.”
verses.kjv: While he answered for himself, Neither against the law of the Jews, neither against the temple, nor yet against Cæsar, have I offended any thing at all.
verses.bsb: Then Paul made his defense: “I have committed no offense against the law of the Jews or against the temple or against Caesar.”

verses.esv: But Festus, wishing to do the Jews a favor, said to Paul, “Do you wish to go up to Jerusalem and there be tried on these charges before me?”
verses.kjv: But Festus, willing to do the Jews a pleasure, answered Paul, and said, Wilt thou go up to Jerusalem, and there be judged of these things before me?
verses.bsb: But Festus, wishing to do the Jews a favor, said to Paul, “Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem to stand trial before me on these charges?”

verses.esv: But Paul said, “I am standing before Caesar's tribunal, where I ought to be tried. To the Jews I have done no wrong, as you yourself know very well.
verses.kjv: Then said Paul, I stand at Cæsar’s judgment seat, where I ought to be judged: to the Jews have I done no wrong, as thou very well knowest.
verses.bsb: Paul replied, “I am standing before the judgment seat of Caesar, where I ought to be tried. I have done nothing wrong to the Jews, as you yourself know very well.

verses.esv: If then I am a wrongdoer and have committed anything for which I deserve to die, I do not seek to escape death. But if there is nothing to their charges against me, no one can give me up to them. I appeal to Caesar.”
verses.kjv: For if I be an offender, or have committed any thing worthy of death, I refuse not to die: but if there be none of these things whereof these accuse me, no man may deliver me unto them. I appeal unto Cæsar.
verses.bsb: If, however, I am guilty of anything worthy of death, I do not refuse to die. But if there is no truth to their accusations against me, no one has the right to hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar!”

verses.esv: Then Festus, when he had conferred with his council, answered, “To Caesar you have appealed; to Caesar you shall go.”
verses.kjv: Then Festus, when he had conferred with the council, answered, Hast thou appealed unto Cæsar? unto Cæsar shalt thou go.
verses.bsb: Then Festus conferred with his council and replied, “You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you will go!”

verses.esv: Now when some days had passed, Agrippa the king and Bernice arrived at Caesarea and greeted Festus.
verses.kjv: And after certain days king Agrippa and Bernice came unto Cæsarea to salute Festus.
verses.bsb: After several days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice came down to Caesarea to pay their respects to Festus.

verses.esv: And as they stayed there many days, Festus laid Paul's case before the king, saying, “There is a man left prisoner by Felix,
verses.kjv: And when they had been there many days, Festus declared Paul’s cause unto the king, saying, There is a certain man left in bonds by Felix:
verses.bsb: Since they were staying several days, Festus laid out Paul’s case before the king: “There is a certain man whom Felix left in prison.

verses.esv: and when I was at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews laid out their case against him, asking for a sentence of condemnation against him.
verses.kjv: About whom, when I was at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews informed me, desiring to have judgment against him.
verses.bsb: While I was in Jerusalem, the chief priests and elders of the Jews presented their case and requested a judgment against him.

verses.esv: I answered them that it was not the custom of the Romans to give up anyone before the accused met the accusers face to face and had opportunity to make his defense concerning the charge laid against him.
verses.kjv: To whom I answered, It is not the manner of the Romans to deliver any man to die, before that he which is accused have the accusers face to face, and have licence to answer for himself concerning the crime laid against him.
verses.bsb: I told them that it is not the Roman custom to hand a man over before he has had an opportunity to face his accusers and defend himself against their charges.

verses.esv: So when they came together here, I made no delay, but on the next day took my seat on the tribunal and ordered the man to be brought.
verses.kjv: Therefore, when they were come hither, without any delay on the morrow I sat on the judgment seat, and commanded the man to be brought forth.
verses.bsb: So when they came here with me, I did not delay. The next day I sat on the judgment seat and ordered that the man be brought in.

verses.esv: When the accusers stood up, they brought no charge in his case of such evils as I supposed.
verses.kjv: Against whom when the accusers stood up, they brought none accusation of such things as I supposed:
verses.bsb: But when his accusers rose to speak, they did not charge him with any of the crimes I had expected.

verses.esv: Rather they had certain points of dispute with him about their own religion and about a certain Jesus, who was dead, but whom Paul asserted to be alive.
verses.kjv: But had certain questions against him of their own superstition, and of one Jesus, which was dead, whom Paul affirmed to be alive.
verses.bsb: They only had some contentions with him regarding their own religion and a certain Jesus who had died, but whom Paul affirmed to be alive.

verses.esv: Being at a loss how to investigate these questions, I asked whether he wanted to go to Jerusalem and be tried there regarding them.
verses.kjv: And because I doubted of such manner of questions, I asked him whether he would go to Jerusalem, and there be judged of these matters.
verses.bsb: Since I was at a loss as to how to investigate these matters, I asked if he was willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried there on these charges.

verses.esv: But when Paul had appealed to be kept in custody for the decision of the emperor, I ordered him to be held until I could send him to Caesar.”
verses.kjv: But when Paul had appealed to be reserved unto the hearing of Augustus, I commanded him to be kept till I might send him to Cæsar.
verses.bsb: But when Paul appealed to be held over for the decision of the Emperor, I ordered that he be held until I could send him to Caesar.”

verses.esv: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would like to hear the man myself.” “Tomorrow,” said he, “you will hear him.”
verses.kjv: Then Agrippa said unto Festus, I would also hear the man myself. To morrow, said he, thou shalt hear him.
verses.bsb: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would like to hear this man myself.”  “Tomorrow you will hear him,” Festus declared.

verses.esv: So on the next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great pomp, and they entered the audience hall with the military tribunes and the prominent men of the city. Then, at the command of Festus, Paul was brought in.
verses.kjv: And on the morrow, when Agrippa was come, and Bernice, with great pomp, and was entered into the place of hearing, with the chief captains, and principal men of the city, at Festus’ commandment Paul was brought forth.
verses.bsb: The next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great pomp and entered the auditorium, along with the commanders and leading men of the city. And Festus ordered that Paul be brought in.

verses.esv: And Festus said, “King Agrippa and all who are present with us, you see this man about whom the whole Jewish people petitioned me, both in Jerusalem and here, shouting that he ought not to live any longer.
verses.kjv: And Festus said, King Agrippa, and all men which are here present with us, ye see this man, about whom all the multitude of the Jews have dealt with me, both at Jerusalem, and also here, crying that he ought not to live any longer.
verses.bsb: Then Festus said, “King Agrippa and all who are present with us, you see this man. The whole Jewish community has petitioned me about him, both here and in Jerusalem, crying out that he ought not to live any longer.

verses.esv: But I found that he had done nothing deserving death. And as he himself appealed to the emperor, I decided to go ahead and send him.
verses.kjv: But when I found that he had committed nothing worthy of death, and that he himself hath appealed to Augustus, I have determined to send him.
verses.bsb: But I found he had done nothing worthy of death, and since he has now appealed to the Emperor, I decided to send him.

verses.esv: But I have nothing definite to write to my lord about him. Therefore I have brought him before you all, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that, after we have examined him, I may have something to write.
verses.kjv: Of whom I have no certain thing to write unto my lord. Wherefore I have brought him forth before you, and specially before thee, O king Agrippa, that, after examination had, I might have somewhat to write.
verses.bsb: I have nothing definite to write to our sovereign one about him. Therefore I have brought him before all of you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after this inquiry I may have something to write.

verses.esv: For it seems to me unreasonable, in sending a prisoner, not to indicate the charges against him.”
verses.kjv: For it seemeth to me unreasonable to send a prisoner, and not withal to signify the crimes laid against him.
verses.bsb: For it seems unreasonable to me to send on a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.”

verses.esv: So Agrippa said to Paul, “You have permission to speak for yourself.” Then Paul stretched out his hand and made his defense:
verses.kjv: THEN Agrippa said unto Paul, Thou art permitted to speak for thyself. Then Paul stretched forth the hand, and answered for himself:
verses.bsb: Agrippa said to Paul, “You have permission to speak for yourself.”  Then Paul stretched out his hand and began his defense:

verses.esv: “I consider myself fortunate that it is before you, King Agrippa, I am going to make my defense today against all the accusations of the Jews,
verses.kjv: I think myself happy, king Agrippa, because I shall answer for myself this day before thee touching all the things whereof I am accused of the Jews:
verses.bsb: “King Agrippa, I consider myself fortunate to stand before you today to defend myself against all the accusations of the Jews,

verses.esv: especially because you are familiar with all the customs and controversies of the Jews. Therefore I beg you to listen to me patiently.
verses.kjv: Especially because I know thee to be expert in all customs and questions which are among the Jews: wherefore I beseech thee to hear me patiently.
verses.bsb: especially since you are acquainted with all the Jewish customs and controversies. I beg you, therefore, to listen to me patiently.

verses.esv: “My manner of life from my youth, spent from the beginning among my own nation and in Jerusalem, is known by all the Jews.
verses.kjv: My manner of life from my youth, which was at the first among mine own nation at Jerusalem, know all the Jews;
verses.bsb: Surely all the Jews know how I have lived from the earliest days of my youth, among my own people and in Jerusalem.

verses.esv: They have known for a long time, if they are willing to testify, that according to the strictest party of our religion I have lived as a Pharisee.
verses.kjv: Which knew me from the beginning, if they would testify, that after the most straitest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.
verses.bsb: They have known me for a long time and can testify, if they are willing, that I lived as a Pharisee, adhering to the strictest sect of our religion.

verses.esv: And now I stand here on trial because of my hope in the promise made by God to our fathers,
verses.kjv: And now I stand and am judged for the hope of the promise made of God unto our fathers:
verses.bsb: And now I stand on trial because of my hope in the promise that God made to our fathers,

verses.esv: to which our twelve tribes hope to attain, as they earnestly worship night and day. And for this hope I am accused by Jews, O king!
verses.kjv: Unto which promise our twelve tribes, instantly serving God day and night, hope to come. For which hope’s sake, king Agrippa, I am accused of the Jews.
verses.bsb: the promise our twelve tribes are hoping to realize as they earnestly serve God day and night. It is because of this hope, O king, that I am accused by the Jews.

verses.esv: Why is it thought incredible by any of you that God raises the dead?
verses.kjv: Why should it be thought a thing incredible with you, that God should raise the dead?
verses.bsb: Why would any of you consider it incredible that God raises the dead?

verses.esv: “I myself was convinced that I ought to do many things in opposing the name of Jesus of Nazareth.
verses.kjv: I verily thought with myself, that I ought to do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth.
verses.bsb: So then, I too was convinced that I ought to do all I could to oppose the name of Jesus of Nazareth.

verses.esv: And I did so in Jerusalem. I not only locked up many of the saints in prison after receiving authority from the chief priests, but when they were put to death I cast my vote against them.
verses.kjv: Which thing I also did in Jerusalem: and many of the saints did I shut up in prison, having received authority from the chief priests; and when they were put to death, I gave my voice against them.
verses.bsb: And that is what I did in Jerusalem. With authority from the chief priests I put many of the saints in prison, and when they were condemned to death, I cast my vote against them.

verses.esv: And I punished them often in all the synagogues and tried to make them blaspheme, and in raging fury against them I persecuted them even to foreign cities.
verses.kjv: And I punished them oft in every synagogue, and compelled them to blaspheme; and being exceedingly mad against them, I persecuted them even unto strange cities.
verses.bsb: I frequently had them punished in the synagogues, and I tried to make them blaspheme. In my raging fury against them, I even went to foreign cities to persecute them.

verses.esv: “In this connection I journeyed to Damascus with the authority and commission of the chief priests.
verses.kjv: Whereupon as I went to Damascus with authority and commission from the chief priests,
verses.bsb: In this pursuit I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and commission of the chief priests.

verses.esv: At midday, O king, I saw on the way a light from heaven, brighter than the sun, that shone around me and those who journeyed with me.
verses.kjv: At midday, O king, I saw in the way a light from heaven, above the brightness of the sun, shining round about me and them which journeyed with me.
verses.bsb: About noon, O king, as I was on the road, I saw a light from heaven, brighter than the sun, shining around me and my companions.

verses.esv: And when we had all fallen to the ground, I heard a voice saying to me in the Hebrew language, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me? It is hard for you to kick against the goads.’
verses.kjv: And when we were all fallen to the earth, I heard a voice speaking unto me, and saying in the Hebrew tongue, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? it is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.
verses.bsb: We all fell to the ground, and I heard a voice say to me in Hebrew, ‘Saul, Saul, why do you persecute Me? It is hard for you to kick against the goads.’

verses.esv: And I said, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ And the Lord said, ‘I am Jesus whom you are persecuting.
verses.kjv: And I said, Who art thou, Lord? And he said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest.
verses.bsb: ‘Who are You, Lord?’ I asked.  ‘I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting,’ the Lord replied.

verses.esv: But rise and stand upon your feet, for I have appeared to you for this purpose, to appoint you as a servant and witness to the things in which you have seen me and to those in which I will appear to you,
verses.kjv: But rise, and stand upon thy feet: for I have appeared unto thee for this purpose, to make thee a minister and a witness both of these things which thou hast seen, and of those things in the which I will appear unto thee;
verses.bsb: ‘But get up and stand on your feet. For I have appeared to you to appoint you as a servant and as a witness of what you have seen from Me and what I will show you.

verses.esv: delivering you from your people and from the Gentiles—to whom I am sending you
verses.kjv: Delivering thee from the people, and from the Gentiles, unto whom now I send thee,
verses.bsb: I will rescue you from your own people and from the Gentiles. I am sending you to them

verses.esv: to open their eyes, so that they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins and a place among those who are sanctified by faith in me.’
verses.kjv: To open their eyes, and to turn them from darkness to light, and from the power of Satan unto God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins, and inheritance among them which are sanctified by faith that is in me.
verses.bsb: to open their eyes, so that they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins and an inheritance among those sanctified by faith in Me.’

verses.esv: “Therefore, O King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision,
verses.kjv: Whereupon, O king Agrippa, I was not disobedient unto the heavenly vision:
verses.bsb: So then, King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision.

verses.esv: but declared first to those in Damascus, then in Jerusalem and throughout all the region of Judea, and also to the Gentiles, that they should repent and turn to God, performing deeds in keeping with their repentance.
verses.kjv: But shewed first unto them of Damascus, and at Jerusalem, and throughout all the coasts of Judæa, and then to the Gentiles, that they should repent and turn to God, and do works meet for repentance.
verses.bsb: First to those in Damascus and Jerusalem, then to everyone in the region of Judea, and then to the Gentiles, I declared that they should repent and turn to God, performing deeds worthy of their repentance.

verses.esv: For this reason the Jews seized me in the temple and tried to kill me.
verses.kjv: For these causes the Jews caught me in the temple, and went about to kill me.
verses.bsb: For this reason the Jews seized me in the temple courts and tried to kill me.

verses.esv: To this day I have had the help that comes from God, and so I stand here testifying both to small and great, saying nothing but what the prophets and Moses said would come to pass:
verses.kjv: Having therefore obtained help of God, I continue unto this day, witnessing both to small and great, saying none other things than those which the prophets and Moses did say should come:
verses.bsb: But I have had God’s help to this day, and I stand here to testify to small and great alike. I am saying nothing beyond what the prophets and Moses said would happen:

verses.esv: that the Christ must suffer and that, by being the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to our people and to the Gentiles.”
verses.kjv: That Christ should suffer, and that he should be the first that should rise from the dead, and should shew light unto the people, and to the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: that the Christ would suffer, and as the first to rise from the dead, would proclaim light to our people and to the Gentiles.”

verses.esv: And as he was saying these things in his defense, Festus said with a loud voice, “Paul, you are out of your mind; your great learning is driving you out of your mind.”
verses.kjv: And as he thus spake for himself, Festus said with a loud voice, Paul, thou art beside thyself; much learning doth make thee mad.
verses.bsb: At this stage of Paul’s defense, Festus exclaimed in a loud voice, “You are insane, Paul! Your great learning is driving you to madness!”

verses.esv: But Paul said, “I am not out of my mind, most excellent Festus, but I am speaking true and rational words.
verses.kjv: But he said, I am not mad, most noble Festus; but speak forth the words of truth and soberness.
verses.bsb: But Paul answered, “I am not insane, most excellent Festus; I am speaking words of truth and sobriety.

verses.esv: For the king knows about these things, and to him I speak boldly. For I am persuaded that none of these things has escaped his notice, for this has not been done in a corner.
verses.kjv: For the king knoweth of these things, before whom also I speak freely: for I am persuaded that none of these things are hidden from him; for this thing was not done in a corner.
verses.bsb: For the king knows about these matters, and I can speak freely to him. I am confident that none of this has escaped his notice, because it was not done in a corner.

verses.esv: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know that you believe.”
verses.kjv: King Agrippa, believest thou the prophets? I know that thou believest.
verses.bsb: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know you do.”

verses.esv: And Agrippa said to Paul, “In a short time would you persuade me to be a Christian?”
verses.kjv: Then Agrippa said unto Paul, Almost thou persuadest me to be a Christian.
verses.bsb: Then Agrippa said to Paul, “Can you persuade me in such a short time to become a Christian?”

verses.esv: And Paul said, “Whether short or long, I would to God that not only you but also all who hear me this day might become such as I am—except for these chains.”
verses.kjv: And Paul said, I would to God, that not only thou, but also all that hear me this day, were both almost, and altogether such as I am, except these bonds.
verses.bsb: “Short time or long,” Paul replied, “I wish to God that not only you but all who hear me this day may become what I am, except for these chains.”

verses.esv: Then the king rose, and the governor and Bernice and those who were sitting with them.
verses.kjv: And when he had thus spoken, the king rose up, and the governor, and Bernice, and they that sat with them:
verses.bsb: Then the king and the governor rose, along with Bernice and those seated with them.

verses.esv: And when they had withdrawn, they said to one another, “This man is doing nothing to deserve death or imprisonment.”
verses.kjv: And when they were gone aside, they talked between themselves, saying, This man doeth nothing worthy of death or of bonds.
verses.bsb: On their way out, they said to one another, “This man has done nothing worthy of death or imprisonment.”

verses.esv: And Agrippa said to Festus, “This man could have been set free if he had not appealed to Caesar.”
verses.kjv: Then said Agrippa unto Festus, This man might have been set at liberty, if he had not appealed unto Cæsar.
verses.bsb: And Agrippa said to Festus, “This man could have been released if he had not appealed to Caesar.”

verses.esv: And when it was decided that we should sail for Italy, they delivered Paul and some other prisoners to a centurion of the Augustan Cohort named Julius.
verses.kjv: AND when it was determined that we should sail into Italy, they delivered Paul and certain other prisoners unto one named Julius, a centurion of Augustus’ band.
verses.bsb: When it was decided that we would sail for Italy, Paul and some other prisoners were handed over to a centurion named Julius, who belonged to the Imperial Regiment.

verses.esv: And embarking in a ship of Adramyttium, which was about to sail to the ports along the coast of Asia, we put to sea, accompanied by Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica.
verses.kjv: And entering into a ship of Adramyttium, we launched, meaning to sail by the coasts of Asia; one Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica, being with us.
verses.bsb: We boarded an Adramyttian ship about to sail for ports along the coast of Asia, and we put out to sea. Aristarchus, a Macedonian from Thessalonica, was with us.

verses.esv: The next day we put in at Sidon. And Julius treated Paul kindly and gave him leave to go to his friends and be cared for.
verses.kjv: And the next day we touched at Sidon. And Julius courteously entreated Paul, and gave him liberty to go unto his friends to refresh himself.
verses.bsb: The next day we landed at Sidon, and Julius treated Paul with consideration, allowing him to visit his friends and receive their care.

verses.esv: And putting out to sea from there we sailed under the lee of Cyprus, because the winds were against us.
verses.kjv: And when we had launched from thence, we sailed under Cyprus, because the winds were contrary.
verses.bsb: After putting out from there, we sailed to the lee of Cyprus because the winds were against us.

verses.esv: And when we had sailed across the open sea along the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, we came to Myra in Lycia.
verses.kjv: And when we had sailed over the sea of Cilicia and Pamphylia, we came to Myra, a city of Lycia.
verses.bsb: And when we had sailed across the open sea off the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, we came to Myra in Lycia.

verses.esv: There the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing for Italy and put us on board.
verses.kjv: And there the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing into Italy; and he put us therein.
verses.bsb: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship sailing for Italy and put us on board.

verses.esv: We sailed slowly for a number of days and arrived with difficulty off Cnidus, and as the wind did not allow us to go farther, we sailed under the lee of Crete off Salmone.
verses.kjv: And when we had sailed slowly many days, and scarce were come over against Cnidus, the wind not suffering us, we sailed under Crete, over against Salmone;
verses.bsb: After sailing slowly for many days, we arrived off Cnidus. When the wind impeded us, we sailed to the lee of Crete, opposite Salmone.

verses.esv: Coasting along it with difficulty, we came to a place called Fair Havens, near which was the city of Lasea.
verses.kjv: And, hardly passing it, came unto a place which is called The fair havens; nigh whereunto was the city of Lasea.
verses.bsb: After we had moved along the coast with difficulty, we came to a place called Fair Havens, near the town of Lasea.

verses.esv: Since much time had passed, and the voyage was now dangerous because even the Fast was already over, Paul advised them,
verses.kjv: Now when much time was spent, and when sailing was now dangerous, because the fast was now already past, Paul admonished them,
verses.bsb: By now much time had passed, and the voyage had already become dangerous because it was after the Fast. So Paul advised them,

verses.esv: saying, “Sirs, I perceive that the voyage will be with injury and much loss, not only of the cargo and the ship, but also of our lives.”
verses.kjv: And said unto them, Sirs, I perceive that this voyage will be with hurt and much damage, not only of the lading and ship, but also of our lives.
verses.bsb: “Men, I can see that our voyage will be filled with disaster and great loss, not only to ship and cargo, but to our own lives as well.”

verses.esv: But the centurion paid more attention to the pilot and to the owner of the ship than to what Paul said.
verses.kjv: Nevertheless the centurion believed the master and the owner of the ship, more than those things which were spoken by Paul.
verses.bsb: But contrary to Paul’s advice, the centurion was persuaded by the pilot and by the owner of the ship.

verses.esv: And because the harbor was not suitable to spend the winter in, the majority decided to put out to sea from there, on the chance that somehow they could reach Phoenix, a harbor of Crete, facing both southwest and northwest, and spend the winter there.
verses.kjv: And because the haven was not commodious to winter in, the more part advised to depart thence also, if by any means they might attain to Phenice, and there to winter; which is an haven of Crete, and lieth toward the south west and north west.
verses.bsb: Since the harbor was unsuitable to winter in, the majority decided to sail on, if somehow they could reach Phoenix to winter there. Phoenix was a harbor in Crete facing both southwest and northwest.

verses.esv: Now when the south wind blew gently, supposing that they had obtained their purpose, they weighed anchor and sailed along Crete, close to the shore.
verses.kjv: And when the south wind blew softly, supposing that they had obtained their purpose, loosing thence, they sailed close by Crete.
verses.bsb: When a gentle south wind began to blow, they thought they had their opportunity. So they weighed anchor and sailed along, hugging the coast of Crete.

verses.esv: But soon a tempestuous wind, called the northeaster, struck down from the land.
verses.kjv: But not long after there arose against it a tempestuous wind, called Euroclydon.
verses.bsb: But it was not long before a cyclone called the Northeaster swept down across the island.

verses.esv: And when the ship was caught and could not face the wind, we gave way to it and were driven along.
verses.kjv: And when the ship was caught, and could not bear up into the wind, we let her drive.
verses.bsb: Unable to head into the wind, the ship was caught up. So we gave way and let ourselves be driven along.

verses.esv: Running under the lee of a small island called Cauda, we managed with difficulty to secure the ship's boat.
verses.kjv: And running under a certain island which is called Clauda, we had much work to come by the boat:
verses.bsb: Passing to the lee of a small island called Cauda, we barely managed to secure the lifeboat.

verses.esv: After hoisting it up, they used supports to undergird the ship. Then, fearing that they would run aground on the Syrtis, they lowered the gear, and thus they were driven along.
verses.kjv: Which when they had taken up, they used helps, undergirding the ship; and, fearing lest they should fall into the quicksands, strake sail, and so were driven.
verses.bsb: After hoisting it up, the crew used ropes to undergird the ship. And fearing that they would run aground on the sandbars of Syrtis, they lowered the sea anchor and were driven along.

verses.esv: Since we were violently storm-tossed, they began the next day to jettison the cargo.
verses.kjv: And we being exceedingly tossed with a tempest, the next day they lightened the ship;
verses.bsb: We were tossed so violently that the next day the men began to jettison the cargo.

verses.esv: And on the third day they threw the ship's tackle overboard with their own hands.
verses.kjv: And the third day we cast out with our own hands the tackling of the ship.
verses.bsb: On the third day, they threw the ship’s tackle overboard with their own hands.

verses.esv: When neither sun nor stars appeared for many days, and no small tempest lay on us, all hope of our being saved was at last abandoned.
verses.kjv: And when neither sun nor stars in many days appeared, and no small tempest lay on us, all hope that we should be saved was then taken away.
verses.bsb: When neither sun nor stars appeared for many days and the great storm continued to batter us, we abandoned all hope of being saved.

verses.esv: Since they had been without food for a long time, Paul stood up among them and said, “Men, you should have listened to me and not have set sail from Crete and incurred this injury and loss.
verses.kjv: But after long abstinence Paul stood forth in the midst of them, and said, Sirs, ye should have hearkened unto me, and not have loosed from Crete, and to have gained this harm and loss.
verses.bsb: After the men had gone a long time without food, Paul stood up among them and said, “Men, you should have followed my advice not to sail from Crete. Then you would have averted this disaster and loss.

verses.esv: Yet now I urge you to take heart, for there will be no loss of life among you, but only of the ship.
verses.kjv: And now I exhort you to be of good cheer: for there shall be no loss of any man’s life among you, but of the ship.
verses.bsb: But now I urge you to keep up your courage, because you will not experience any loss of life, but only of the ship.

verses.esv: For this very night there stood before me an angel of the God to whom I belong and whom I worship,
verses.kjv: For there stood by me this night the angel of God, whose I am, and whom I serve,
verses.bsb: For just last night an angel of God, whose I am and whom I serve, stood beside me

verses.esv: and he said, ‘Do not be afraid, Paul; you must stand before Caesar. And behold, God has granted you all those who sail with you.’
verses.kjv: Saying, Fear not, Paul; thou must be brought before Cæsar: and, lo, God hath given thee all them that sail with thee.
verses.bsb: and said, ‘Do not be afraid, Paul; you must stand before Caesar. And look, God has granted you the lives of all who sail with you.’

verses.esv: So take heart, men, for I have faith in God that it will be exactly as I have been told.
verses.kjv: Wherefore, sirs, be of good cheer: for I believe God, that it shall be even as it was told me.
verses.bsb: So take courage, men, for I believe God that it will happen just as He told me.

verses.esv: But we must run aground on some island.”
verses.kjv: Howbeit we must be cast upon a certain island.
verses.bsb: However, we must run aground on some island.”

verses.esv: When the fourteenth night had come, as we were being driven across the Adriatic Sea, about midnight the sailors suspected that they were nearing land.
verses.kjv: But when the fourteenth night was come, as we were driven up and down in Adria, about midnight the shipmen deemed that they drew near to some country;
verses.bsb: On the fourteenth night we were still being driven across the Adriatic Sea. About midnight the sailors sensed they were approaching land.

verses.esv: So they took a sounding and found twenty fathoms. A little farther on they took a sounding again and found fifteen fathoms.
verses.kjv: And sounded, and found it twenty fathoms: and when they had gone a little further, they sounded again, and found it fifteen fathoms.
verses.bsb: They took soundings and found that the water was twenty fathoms deep. Going a little farther, they took another set of soundings that read fifteen fathoms.

verses.esv: And fearing that we might run on the rocks, they let down four anchors from the stern and prayed for day to come.
verses.kjv: Then fearing lest we should have fallen upon rocks, they cast four anchors out of the stern, and wished for the day.
verses.bsb: Fearing that we would run aground on the rocks, they dropped four anchors from the stern and prayed for daybreak.

verses.esv: And as the sailors were seeking to escape from the ship, and had lowered the ship's boat into the sea under pretense of laying out anchors from the bow,
verses.kjv: And as the shipmen were about to flee out of the ship, when they had let down the boat into the sea, under colour as though they would have cast anchors out of the foreship,
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the sailors attempted to escape from the ship. Pretending to lower anchors from the bow, they let the lifeboat down into the sea.

verses.esv: Paul said to the centurion and the soldiers, “Unless these men stay in the ship, you cannot be saved.”
verses.kjv: Paul said to the centurion and to the soldiers, Except these abide in the ship, ye cannot be saved.
verses.bsb: But Paul said to the centurion and the soldiers, “Unless these men remain with the ship, you cannot be saved.”

verses.esv: Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the ship's boat and let it go.
verses.kjv: Then the soldiers cut off the ropes of the boat, and let her fall off.
verses.bsb: So the soldiers cut the ropes to the lifeboat and set it adrift.

verses.esv: As day was about to dawn, Paul urged them all to take some food, saying, “Today is the fourteenth day that you have continued in suspense and without food, having taken nothing.
verses.kjv: And while the day was coming on, Paul besought them all to take meat, saying, This day is the fourteenth day that ye have tarried and continued fasting, having taken nothing.
verses.bsb: Right up to daybreak, Paul kept urging them all to eat: “Today is your fourteenth day in constant suspense, without taking any food.

verses.esv: Therefore I urge you to take some food. For it will give you strength, for not a hair is to perish from the head of any of you.”
verses.kjv: Wherefore I pray you to take some meat: for this is for your health: for there shall not an hair fall from the head of any of you.
verses.bsb: So for your own preservation, I urge you to eat something, because not a single hair of your head will be lost.”

verses.esv: And when he had said these things, he took bread, and giving thanks to God in the presence of all he broke it and began to eat.
verses.kjv: And when he had thus spoken, he took bread, and gave thanks to God in presence of them all: and when he had broken it, he began to eat.
verses.bsb: After he had said this, Paul took bread and gave thanks to God in front of them all. Then he broke it and began to eat.

verses.esv: Then they all were encouraged and ate some food themselves.
verses.kjv: Then were they all of good cheer, and they also took some meat.
verses.bsb: They were all encouraged and took some food themselves.

verses.esv: (We were in all 276 persons in the ship.)
verses.kjv: And we were in all in the ship two hundred threescore and sixteen souls.
verses.bsb: In all, there were 276 of us on board.

verses.esv: And when they had eaten enough, they lightened the ship, throwing out the wheat into the sea.
verses.kjv: And when they had eaten enough, they lightened the ship, and cast out the wheat into the sea.
verses.bsb: After the men had eaten their fill, they lightened the ship by throwing the grain into the sea.

verses.esv: Now when it was day, they did not recognize the land, but they noticed a bay with a beach, on which they planned if possible to run the ship ashore.
verses.kjv: And when it was day, they knew not the land: but they discovered a certain creek with a shore, into the which they were minded, if it were possible, to thrust in the ship.
verses.bsb: When daylight came, they did not recognize the land, but they sighted a bay with a sandy beach, where they decided to run the ship aground if they could.

verses.esv: So they cast off the anchors and left them in the sea, at the same time loosening the ropes that tied the rudders. Then hoisting the foresail to the wind they made for the beach.
verses.kjv: And when they had taken up the anchors, they committed themselves unto the sea, and loosed the rudder bands, and hoised up the mainsail to the wind, and made toward shore.
verses.bsb: Cutting away the anchors, they left them in the sea as they loosened the ropes that held the rudders. Then they hoisted the foresail to the wind and made for the beach.

verses.esv: But striking a reef, they ran the vessel aground. The bow stuck and remained immovable, and the stern was being broken up by the surf.
verses.kjv: And falling into a place where two seas met, they ran the ship aground; and the forepart stuck fast, and remained unmoveable, but the hinder part was broken with the violence of the waves.
verses.bsb: But the vessel struck a sandbar and ran aground. The bow stuck fast and would not move, and the stern was being broken up by the pounding of the waves.

verses.esv: The soldiers' plan was to kill the prisoners, lest any should swim away and escape.
verses.kjv: And the soldiers’ counsel was to kill the prisoners, lest any of them should swim out, and escape.
verses.bsb: The soldiers planned to kill the prisoners so none of them could swim to freedom.

verses.esv: But the centurion, wishing to save Paul, kept them from carrying out their plan. He ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and make for the land,
verses.kjv: But the centurion, willing to save Paul, kept them from their purpose; and commanded that they which could swim should cast themselves first into the sea, and get to land:
verses.bsb: But the centurion, wanting to spare Paul’s life, thwarted their plan. He commanded those who could swim to jump overboard first and get to land.

verses.esv: and the rest on planks or on pieces of the ship. And so it was that all were brought safely to land.
verses.kjv: And the rest, some on boards, and some on broken pieces of the ship. And so it came to pass, that they escaped all safe to land.
verses.bsb: The rest were to follow on planks and various parts of the ship. In this way everyone was brought safely to land.

verses.esv: After we were brought safely through, we then learned that the island was called Malta.
verses.kjv: AND when they were escaped, then they knew that the island was called Melita.
verses.bsb: Once we were safely ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.

verses.esv: The native people showed us unusual kindness, for they kindled a fire and welcomed us all, because it had begun to rain and was cold.
verses.kjv: And the barbarous people shewed us no little kindness: for they kindled a fire, and received us every one, because of the present rain, and because of the cold.
